2DOxidationSetUp.description: Sets the parameters for the meshing, time-stepping, diffusion solver, and mechanics solver for 2D oxidation simulations. Used when 2D oxidation is required to modify solver and meshing parameters for accurate oxidation modeling.

AdvancedCalibration.description: Sources the Advanced Calibration parameter file for the specified Sentaurus Process release version and optional material. Sets calibrated diffusion, defect, and oxidation parameters for silicon, SiGe, germanium, and 4H-SiC materials. Must be called before the process description.

parameters.name: version
parameters.desc: Release version string (e.g., 2022.03, 2021.06). Omit for the default version.

parameters.name: material
parameters.desc: Target material: Si (default), SiGe, Ge, 4H-SiC, or SiC.

AdvancedPowerDeviceMode.description: Activates power device simulation mode as part of Advanced Calibration. Sets specific parameter settings optimized for power device simulations such as IGBT and MOSFET structures. For details, see the Advanced Calibration for Process Simulation User Guide.

AdvancedPowerDeviceModeReset.description: Resets the power device simulation parameters that were set by AdvancedPowerDeviceMode back to their default values.

Arr.description: Creates an Arrhenius expression of the form A*exp(-Ea/kT). This is a shorthand alias for the Arrhenius command, commonly used within Tcl expressions and pdbSet parameter specifications.

parameters.name: <prefactor>
parameters.desc: Prefactor A of the Arrhenius expression.

parameters.name: <activation_energy>
parameters.desc: Activation energy Ea in eV.

ArrBreak.description: Creates two Arrhenius expressions with a break temperature. The first expression applies below the break temperature; the second applies at or above it.

parameters.name: <prefactor1>
parameters.desc: Prefactor of the first Arrhenius expression (low temperature).

parameters.name: <Ea1>
parameters.desc: Activation energy [eV] of the first Arrhenius expression.

parameters.name: <prefactor2>
parameters.desc: Prefactor of the second Arrhenius expression (high temperature).

parameters.name: <Ea2>
parameters.desc: Activation energy [eV] of the second Arrhenius expression.

parameters.name: <break_temperature>
parameters.desc: Break temperature in degrees Celsius.

Arrhenius.description: Creates an Arrhenius expression of the form A*exp(-Ea/kT), where the first argument is the prefactor and the second is the activation energy in eV.

parameters.name: <prefactor>
parameters.desc: Prefactor A of the Arrhenius expression.

parameters.name: <activation_energy>
parameters.desc: Activation energy Ea in eV.

Compatibility.description: Applies parameters consistent with the default values of a previous release. Must be the first command in a command file so that all subsequent commands take the compatibility setting into account. Parameters are applied in reverse chronological order from the current release down to the specified release.

parameters.name: <c>
parameters.desc: Release identifier from which to apply parameters (e.g., S-2021.06, 2021.06, Q-2019.12). Aliases are available; the release foundation letter is not required.

Coordinations.Planes.Compatibility.description: Specifies a compatibility mode of the LKMC Coordinations model so that the growth rate matches that of the Coordinations.Planes model. Prints a warning if the Coordinations model has not been enabled.

CutLine2D.description: Computes the slice angle when given a cut line in wafer coordinates defined by two endpoints. Used to determine the proper orientation for wafer-level cuts in 2D simulations.

parameters.name: <x1>
parameters.desc: X-coordinate of the first endpoint of the cutline.

parameters.name: <y1>
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate of the first endpoint of the cutline.

parameters.name: <x2>
parameters.desc: X-coordinate of the second endpoint of the cutline.

parameters.name: <y2>
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate of the second endpoint of the cutline.

DeleteRefinementboxes.description: Deletes a set of refinement boxes whose names match the given Tcl glob pattern.

parameters.name: pattern=
parameters.desc: Tcl glob pattern to match refinement box names (expanded according to standard Tcl rules).

Enu2G.description: Computes the shear modulus from Young's modulus and the Poisson ratio. The same units are assumed for all moduli.

parameters.name: <youngs_modulus>
parameters.desc: Young's modulus (e.g., dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: <poisson_ratio>
parameters.desc: Poisson's ratio (dimensionless).

Enu2K.description: Computes the bulk modulus from Young's modulus and the Poisson ratio. The same units are assumed for all moduli.

parameters.name: <youngs_modulus>
parameters.desc: Young's modulus (e.g., dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: <poisson_ratio>
parameters.desc: Poisson's ratio (dimensionless).

GetMoleFractionFields.description: Returns the constituent element fields of an alloy material for a given material or region with specified mole fractions. Used to set up composition-dependent field values during deposition of alloy layers.

parameters.name: <material_or_region>
parameters.desc: Name of the material or region where the fields are to be evaluated.

parameters.name: <alloy_material>
parameters.desc: Name of an alloy material such as SiGe or InGaAs.

parameters.name: <xMoleFraction>
parameters.desc: Mole fraction value between 0 and 1 for the first constituent.

parameters.name: <yMoleFraction>
parameters.desc: Mole fraction value for the second constituent (required for quaternary materials only).

GetMoleFractionParam.description: Returns the interpolation expression of a given parameter for an alloy material. Does not apply to KMC or MC implantation parameters. The parameter list follows the same hierarchy as the Parameter Database (PDB).

parameters.name: <alloy_material>
parameters.desc: Name of an alloy material such as SiGe or InGaAs.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Hierarchical parameter specification identical to PDB (e.g., Int Cstar, B Segregation).

KG2E.description: Computes Young's modulus from the bulk modulus and the shear modulus. The same units are assumed for all moduli.

parameters.name: <bulk_modulus>
parameters.desc: Bulk modulus (e.g., dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: <shear_modulus>
parameters.desc: Shear modulus (e.g., dyn/cm2).

KG2nu.description: Computes the Poisson ratio from the bulk modulus and the shear modulus. The same units are assumed for all moduli.

parameters.name: <bulk_modulus>
parameters.desc: Bulk modulus (e.g., dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: <shear_modulus>
parameters.desc: Shear modulus (e.g., dyn/cm2).

KMC2PDE.description: Translates atomistic KMC information into continuum five-stream quantities and transfers them to the standard Sentaurus Process mesh. Performs deatomization of particles into concentrations and maps KMC field names to PDE field names.

LogFile.description: Prints messages to the terminal window and to the log file. Supports information-level filtering and control over command printing. Use !PrintCommands to suppress command echo.

parameters.name: <c>
parameters.desc: The message to be printed to the screen and the log file.

parameters.name: IL0|IL1|IL2|IL3
parameters.desc: Information level filter. The message is printed only if the information level is equal to or greater than specified.

parameters.name: PrintCommands
parameters.desc: Controls whether commands are printed to the screen and log file. Use !PrintCommands to suppress. Default: true.

MoleFractionFields.description: Returns the component fields and field values in alloys for a given mole fraction. Used to initialize component fields in alloy materials for init, deposit, diffuse, and temp_ramp commands.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Alloy material name such as SiliconGermanium or AlInGaAs.

parameters.name: <xMoleFraction>
parameters.desc: Required mole fraction value between 0 and 1.

parameters.name: <yMoleFraction>
parameters.desc: Second mole fraction for quaternary III-V materials. Value between 0 and 1.

MoleFractionFields.example:
init fields.values= [MoleFractionFields SiliconGermanium 0.2]
deposit AlInGaAs isotropic thickness= 0.1<um> fields.values= [MoleFractionFields AlInGaAs 0.2 0.1]

PDE2KMC.description: Translates and transfers Sentaurus Process continuum fields to Sentaurus Process KMC. Converts continuum concentrations into suitable particle distributions using the select command with appropriate KMC species names. Called automatically when switching from PDE to KMC solver.

PowerDeviceMode.description: Sets diffusion models to match the pd.fermi model of TSUPREM-4 for power-device applications. Switches on Fermi model, solid solubility, DopantOnly charge, equilibrium activation, and segregation models. Switches off dopant/defect clusters and point-defect equations. Relaxes time-step controls and reduces mesh refinement around interfaces.

RangeRefineboxes.description: Creates a set of refinement boxes based on a mask, a center range, and per-box drange extents. All boxes share global refinement settings, but each can have additional local settings.

parameters.name: boxes=
parameters.desc: Tcl list of Tcl lists; each sublist contains drange and box-specific arguments.

parameters.name: mask=
parameters.desc: Name of the mask under which refinement is applied.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Root name for the set of refinement boxes; each inherits a name <root>_<n>.

parameters.name: range=
parameters.desc: Center of the primary extent for all refinement boxes (e.g., from implant range). Default unit: cm.

parameters.name: drange=
parameters.desc: Half-extent of an individual box: extends from range-drange to range+drange.

RangeRefineboxes.example: RangeRefineboxes name= "RM1" mask= "M1_p" range= $range boxes= {{drange= $sigma xrefine= [expr $sigma/4.0] yrefine= [expr $sigma/4.0]}}

Set3DDeviceMeshMode.description: Sets meshing parameters for device simulation of complex 3D structures with curved surfaces. Configures box-method Delaunay meshing, axis-aligned imprinting, and axis-aligned refinement for improved mesh quality on curved boundaries.
Set3DDeviceMeshMode.example:
Set3DDeviceMeshMode
pdbSet Grid SnMesh Apply.Brep.DelPSC 1
pdbSet Grid SnMesh Apply.Brep.DelPSC.Resolution 1e-2
grid remesh info= 1

Set3DMovingMeshMode.description: Sets meshing parameters for 3D MovingMesh applications. Automatically configures parameters based on structure size and protects small geometric features from removal during geometry cleanup.

parameters.name: <min_feature_size>
parameters.desc: Minimum feature size in um; typically the smallest layer thickness. Default unit: um.

SetAtomistic.description: Switches the simulation domain to the atomistic KMC mode, affecting deposit, diffuse, etch, implant, profile, region, select, strip, and struct commands. If continuum fields exist, automatically calls PDE2KMC to atomize them.

SetDielectricOxidationMode.description: Sets the oxidation mode to grow oxide with a dielectric material on top. Configures boundary conditions at the gas-dielectric interface (Dirichlet or MassTransfer) and the dielectric-oxide interface (Continuous or Segregation). The default boundary conditions are Dirichlet at gas-dielectric and Continuous at dielectric-oxide.

parameters.name: <Dielectric>
parameters.desc: Name of the dielectric material to grow oxide underneath (e.g., Nitride).

parameters.name: <Oxidant>
parameters.desc: Name of the oxidant (e.g., O2, H2O).

parameters.name: Continuous
parameters.desc: Sets continuous boundary conditions at the dielectric-oxide interface.

parameters.name: Segregation
parameters.desc: Sets segregation boundary conditions at the dielectric-oxide interface.

parameters.name: Dirichlet
parameters.desc: Sets Dirichlet boundary conditions at the gas-dielectric interface.

parameters.name: MassTransfer
parameters.desc: Sets mass transfer boundary conditions at the gas-dielectric interface.

SetFastMode.description: Switches off selected simulation steps (diffusion, implantation, mechanics, epitaxy, reactions) to run the simulation quickly for geometry verification. Steps can be individually toggled; different options can be combined except etch.deposit.only.

parameters.name: no.diffusion
parameters.desc: Switches off diffusion (dopant redistribution).

parameters.name: no.epi
parameters.desc: Switches off epitaxy.

parameters.name: no.mechanics
parameters.desc: Switches off mechanics steps.

parameters.name: no.reaction
parameters.desc: Switches off oxidation and silicidation.

parameters.name: skip.diffuse
parameters.desc: Omits all diffuse commands.

parameters.name: skip.implant
parameters.desc: Omits all implant commands.

parameters.name: MC.to.analytical
parameters.desc: Replaces Monte Carlo implantation with analytic implantation.

parameters.name: epi.to.deposit
parameters.desc: Replaces epitaxy with a single deposition step.

parameters.name: etch.deposit.only
parameters.desc: Omits diffuse, implant, and mechanics; omits remeshing except grid remesh.

SetIIIVDiffParams.description: Sets the parameters and callback procedures for diffusion in III-V materials. Since parameter values are not well known for many III-V material types, this command is required to set up necessary parameters for diffusion simulation of specified dopants in III-V materials.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Name of the III-V material (e.g., AlGaN, InGaAs).

parameters.name: <list>
parameters.desc: Optional list of dopants. If omitted, parameters for generic dopants are set.

SetInterfaceInjectionLKMC.description: Controls whether injection of point defects is switched on during lattice kinetic Monte Carlo (LKMC) simulations. Used with epitaxy models for LKMC simulations to enable or disable point-defect injection at material interfaces.

parameters.name: true
parameters.desc: Switches on point-defect injection during LKMC simulations.

parameters.name: false
parameters.desc: Switches off point-defect injection during LKMC simulations.

SetMoleFractionFields.description: Sets the appropriate fields of the constituent elements of an alloy material to a given material or region with specified mole fractions. Useful for setting proper mole fraction after region formation.

parameters.name: <material_or_region>
parameters.desc: Name of the material or region where the fields are to be added.

parameters.name: <alloy_material>
parameters.desc: Name of the alloy material such as SiGe or InGaAs.

parameters.name: <xMoleFraction>
parameters.desc: Required mole fraction between 0 and 1.

parameters.name: <yMoleFraction>
parameters.desc: Second mole fraction for quaternary materials. Between 0 and 1.

SetMoleFractionFields.example:
SetMoleFractionFields SiGe_1 SiGe 0.3
SetMoleFractionFields GaAs_1 InGaAs 0.47
SetMoleFractionFields InAs AlInGaAs 0.2 0.3

SetMultiphaseNickelSilicide.description: Calculates and sets PDB parameters for multiphase nickel silicidation, including Density.Grow and Expansion.Ratio at the nickel-nickel silicide and nickel silicide-silicon interfaces. Uses MassDensity and Multiphase.Reaction.Rate parameters along with initial distribution values of phases. Also sets the multiphase flag and low-temperature diffusion threshold.

SetPerformanceMode.description: Modifies the automatic time-step control method and solver parameters to improve simulation speed. Uses experimental algorithms with more aggressive time-stepping and modified ILS solver settings. Algorithms may change between releases.

parameters.name: CMOS
parameters.desc: Modifies ILS solver settings for CMOS process type.

parameters.name: FinFET
parameters.desc: Modifies ILS solver settings for FinFET process type.

SetPlxList.description: Sets a list of solution and term names to be passed to the WritePlx command. Controls which data fields are written to the output .plx file.

parameters.name: <solution/term names>
parameters.desc: List of solution or term names to include in subsequent WritePlx output.

SetTDRList.description: Sets a list of solution or term names to be included when saving TDR format files. Controls which fields, dopant concentrations, and solution variables are stored in struct TDR output.

parameters.name: Dopants
parameters.desc: Includes total and active dopant concentrations. Use !Dopants to exclude (NetActive still saved). Default: true.

parameters.name: <solution/term names>
parameters.desc: Additional known fields to add to files saved with struct tdr=.

parameters.name: Solutions
parameters.desc: Stores all solution variables needed for restarting. Use !Solutions to switch off all default savings. Default: true.

SetTS4ImplantMode.description: Sets implantation-related parameters and models to match TSUPREM-4. Configures beam dose usage, TSUPREM-4 backscattering model, TS4FluorineMode for BF2 implantation, and optionally the PAI model with TSUPREM-4-compatible settings. Results may differ due to numeric method differences.

parameters.name: Native
parameters.desc: Makes results close to the TSUPREM-4 native implanter.

parameters.name: Taurus
parameters.desc: Makes results close to the Taurus TSUPREM-4 implanter; also enables PAI model.

SetTS4MechanicsMode.description: Sets mechanics-related parameters and models in Sentaurus Process to match TSUPREM-4 settings. Includes viscoelastic model and parameters, elastic moduli, stress relaxation factor, stress smoothing, thermal mismatch coefficients, and backward-compatible oxidation settings.

parameters.desc: Used for backward compatibility with Version A-2008.09 defaults.

parameters.name: advanced
parameters.desc: Sets TSUPREM-4 advanced settings.

SetTS4OxidationMode.description: Sets oxidation-related parameters and models to match TSUPREM-4 settings. Includes settings from SetTS4MechanicsMode plus activation volumes of stress-dependent oxidation (SDO) reaction rate and diffusivity, stress-dependent viscosity, native layer thickness, and stress-dependent oxidation flag. Recommended to call before oxidation steps and SetTS4MechanicsMode after.

parameters.desc: Sets oxidation and mechanics parameters to Version A-2008.09 default values.

parameters.name: advanced
parameters.desc: Sets parameters to TSUPREM-4 advanced settings.

SetTS4PolyMode.description: Sets the related parameters and models for the polycrystalline model in Sentaurus Process to match TSUPREM-4 settings.

SetTemp.description: Sets the temperature value for the simulation. The value is also saved in TDR files. Default unit is degrees Celsius.

parameters.name: <n>
parameters.desc: Temperature value.

parameters.name: <C>
parameters.desc: Celsius unit qualifier (default).

parameters.name: <K>
parameters.desc: Kelvin unit qualifier.

SheetResistance.description: Calculates the sheet resistance and the p-n junction depth of a semiconductor layer in the vertical direction. Can only be used after a diffusion step.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: X cut position for 2D or 3D simulations. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Y cut position for 2D or 3D simulations. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Z cut position for 3D simulations. Default unit: um.

StressDependentSilicidation.description: Enables stress-dependent silicidation for a specified silicide. In this model, reaction rates are normal stress dependent, the diffusivity of the reactant species (SiliconReact) is pressure dependent, and the silicide is allowed to relax.

parameters.name: <c>
parameters.desc: Name of the silicide to use the pressure-dependent model (e.g., NickelSilicide).

UnsetAtomistic.description: Disables the atomistic KMC diffusion model and continues with the PDE solver. Transfers all KMC information to the standard mesh via KMC2PDE, optionally applying the Sano method for adaptive remeshing and particle-to-field conversion.

parameters.name: sano
parameters.desc: Remeshes the finite-element mesh and converts KMC particles to fields using the Sano method.

parameters.name: sano.list=
parameters.desc: List of Sano fields for adaptive remeshing. Default: active dopants.

parameters.name: sano.materials=
parameters.desc: Materials where the Sano method is applied. Default: Silicon.

UnsetDielectricOxidationMode.description: Disables the oxidation mode to grow oxide with a dielectric on top. Deletes all dielectric oxidation-related callback settings, boundary conditions, and parameter settings for the specified dielectric and oxidant combination.

parameters.name: <Dielectric>
parameters.desc: Name of the dielectric material.

parameters.name: <Oxidant>
parameters.desc: Name of the oxidant.

WritePlx.description: Makes a 1D profile along a given cutline and writes a .plx file of the solutions and terms specified by SetPlxList. If no list is provided, only present solution names are written.

parameters.name: <filename>
parameters.desc: Name of the output .plx file. Must be the first argument.

parameters.name: datex
parameters.desc: Outputs dataset names in standard datexcodes.txt format. Use !datex for default naming.

parameters.name: include.interfaces
parameters.desc: Includes interface values with the returned data.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Restricts the plot to the given material only.

parameters.name: only.interfaces
parameters.desc: Returns only interface values, no bulk values.

parameters.name: precision=
parameters.desc: Number of precision digits for floating values in scientific notation. Default: 6.

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Restricts data to the given region. Must not be specified together with material.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: X cut position (2D/3D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Y cut position (2D/3D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Z cut position (3D). Default unit: um.

WritePlx.example:
SetPlxList {BTotal NetActive}
WritePlx n@node@_NMOS_channel.plx y=0.0 Silicon

ambient.signature: ambient name=<c> (add | clear | delete | list | print) [epi | inert | react] [lkmc.atom.name=<c>] [lkmc.mass=<n>[<amu>|<g>]] [lkmc.num.atoms=<i>]
ambient.description: Creates new ambients for material growth reactions such as oxidation or silicidation, or creates a new epi growth mode. Ambient names are referenced in the diffuse, gas_flow, reaction, and temp_ramp commands.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the ambient.

parameters.name: add
parameters.desc: Creates a new ambient.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears all known ambients.

parameters.name: delete
parameters.desc: Deletes the named ambient.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a Tcl list of all available ambients.

parameters.name: print
parameters.desc: Prints all available ambients.

parameters.name: epi
parameters.desc: Creates an epi-type ambient for epitaxial growth modes.

parameters.name: inert
parameters.desc: Creates an inert ambient for gas_flow dilution.

parameters.name: react
parameters.desc: Creates an active ambient for oxidation or silicidation reactions.

parameters.name: lkmc.atom.name=
parameters.desc: Name of the atom deposited by the epi gas (for LKMC models).

parameters.name: lkmc.mass=
parameters.desc: Mass of the gas molecule (default unit: g).

parameters.name: lkmc.num.atoms=
parameters.desc: Number of atoms of lkmc.atom.name per molecule (default: 1).

ambient.name.description: Specifies the name of the ambient gas or environment used in the simulation. The name identifies the ambient for reference in subsequent commands such as diffuse or deposit.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Name of the ambient gas (e.g., O2, N2, H2O).

ambient.name.example:
ambient name= O2
ambient name= N2

ambient.products.description: Specifies the product species generated during the ambient reaction. Used in oxidation and diffusion simulations to define reaction products.

parameters.name: products
parameters.desc: List of product species names.

ambient.rate.description: Specifies the reaction rate expression for the ambient gas. Defines the kinetic rate of the surface reaction during oxidation or deposition.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.desc: Rate expression or coefficients for the ambient reaction.

beam.description: Creates a beam for multiple-beam etching and defines the direction and relative strength of etchant beams. The beam name is referenced in the etch command via the sources argument. Beams are assumed to be collimated.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the beam, referenced by the etch command sources argument.

parameters.name: direction=
parameters.desc: Direction vector {x y} defining the beam incidence angle, auto-normalized.

parameters.name: incidence=
parameters.desc: Angle of incidence in degrees. 0 is vertical; positive is from upper-left.

parameters.name: factor=
parameters.desc: Relative strength factor of this beam compared to others.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a Tcl list of known beams.

beam.example:
beam name= source1 incidence= 0 factor= 1
beam name= source2 incidence= 10 factor= 0.5

beam.dose.description: Queries or sets the beam dose value for implantation simulations. Returns the current dose when used without arguments.

bound.description: Extracts the boundary of a material or region, returns the outline as a list of coordinate pairs, and plots the limits of the regions. Not available for 3D simulations.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Name of the material to extract the boundary from.

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Name of the region to extract the boundary from.

parameters.name: list.all
parameters.desc: Lists all boundary coordinates with no ordering. Default: false.

boundary.description: Controls the conversion of geometric column (GC) structures generated by Sentaurus Process Explorer into the boundary representation (brep) used by Sentaurus Process. Conversions occur when reading a TDR GC file using the init command.

parameters.name: spx.column.size=
parameters.desc: Grid cell size for exporting structure geometry to GC format.

parameters.name: spx.depth.num.cells=
parameters.desc: Number of cells for the depth of the structure. Default: 200.

parameters.name: spx.width.num.cells=
parameters.desc: Number of cells for the width of the structure. Default: 200.

parameters.name: spx2brep.method=
parameters.desc: Conversion method: Advanced (accurate, follows skyline), MLS (level-set smoothing), Standard (fast, blocky), or VBE (volume boundary extraction, very smooth). Default: Standard.

parameters.name: spx2brep.standard.tolerance=
parameters.desc: Smoothing amount for Standard method (0 = no smoothing). Default: 0.0.

parameters.name: spx2brep.mls.cellsize=
parameters.desc: Resolution of cells used by the MLS algorithm (multiple of GC resolution). Default: 4x GC resolution.

parameters.name: spx2brep.mls.decimatebrep
parameters.desc: Performs decimation after MLS processing. Default: false.

parameters.name: spx2brep.mls.splinesmooth
parameters.desc: Applies b-spline filter during MLS conversion. Default: true.

parameters.name: spx2brep.standard.decimation
parameters.desc: Performs decimation after Standard conversion. Default: true.

parameters.name: spx2brep.standard.decimation.accuracy=
parameters.desc: Accuracy for decimation (removal tolerance). Default: 1e-5 um.

boundary.conditions.description: Specifies the boundary conditions applied at the edges of the simulation domain. Controls how the simulator handles domain boundaries for diffusion and mechanics calculations.

parameters.name: conditions
parameters.desc: List of boundary condition specifications (e.g., reflecting, absorbing).

contact.description: Defines new contacts, deletes contacts, and prints contact information for device simulation. Contacts are written to TDR files in the struct command and are only intended for structures written for device simulation. Supports box-type and point-type contacts.

parameters.name: add
parameters.desc: Add to an existing contact instead of overwriting.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clear all contacts or the specified contact.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Print a list of currently defined contacts.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material for the contact. Only surface elements of the specified material are selected.

parameters.name: merge=
parameters.desc: List of contacts to merge into a single electrical contact.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the contact. Must not contain special characters such as underscores.

parameters.name: new.name=
parameters.desc: Rename the contact from name to new.name.

parameters.name: print
parameters.desc: Print contact information.

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Name of a volume region or interface region for the contact.

parameters.name: box
parameters.desc: Define a box-type contact. Mesh is cut at contact borders by default.

parameters.name: point
parameters.desc: Define a point/region-type contact containing all boundary elements of one region.

parameters.name: adjacent.material=
parameters.desc: Adjacent material constraint; only elements at the interface are allowed.

parameters.name: cut.mesh
parameters.desc: Enable mesh cutting at contact borders (default). Use !cut.mesh to disable.

parameters.name: depth=
parameters.desc: Depth of the contact in micrometers.

parameters.name: width=
parameters.desc: Width of the contact in micrometers.

parameters.name: left/right/back/front/top/bottom
parameters.desc: Select outer boundaries where contacts will be placed.

parameters.name: xlo=/xhi=/ylo=/yhi=/zlo=/zhi=
parameters.desc: Box coordinate bounds. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: sidewall
parameters.desc: Allow only surface elements on the external boundary (left/right/front/back).

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Replace the material of the region by Gas in the TDR file for point contacts.

parameters.name: replace.box
parameters.desc: Replace the material by Gas in the TDR file for box contacts.

contact.example: contact name=gate box PolySilicon xlo=$minx-0.005 xhi=$minx+0.005 ylo=@<-sti_width>@ yhi=@sti_width@

contour.description: Plots a contour of the selected variable or named data field at the specified value on a 2D plot. Requires plot.2d to be configured beforehand.

parameters.name: color=
parameters.desc: Line color of the contour (X11 color name).

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the data field to plot. Default: Z_Plot_Var.

parameters.name: print
parameters.desc: Prints contour values instead of plotting (output compatible with xgraph).

parameters.name: value=
parameters.desc: Value at which the contour line should be plotted.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: X-coordinate for 3D contour plane. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate for 3D contour plane. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Z-coordinate for 3D contour plane. Default unit: um.

create.all.masks.description: Creates masks for all layers defined in the IC Workbench (ICWB) layout file. Automatically processes every layer and generates corresponding mask definitions for subsequent deposit and etch operations.

define.description: Defines a Tcl variable that is saved and restored in TDR files. Equivalent to the Tcl set command, except that set variables are not persisted through TDR save/load. The fset command is an alias.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Any user-defined parameter name.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Any number or string value.

define.example:
define LG 0.02
fset SD_L 300
fset M1_left [expr {$SD_L + 25}]

defineproc.description: Defines a Tcl procedure that is saved and restored in TDR files. Equivalent to the Tcl proc command, except that proc-defined procedures are not persisted through TDR save/load. The fproc command is an alias.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the Tcl procedure.

parameters.name: <procedure_arguments>
parameters.desc: List of arguments for the procedure.

parameters.name: <body_of_procedure>
parameters.desc: Body of the Tcl procedure.

deposit.signature: deposit [<material>] [anisotropic | crystal | fill | fourier | isotropic | polygon | trapezoidal] [coord=<n>][<um>] [thickness=<n>][<um>] [1D] [Adaptive] [angle=<n>] [coeffs= {<A0> <A1> ...}] [crystal.rate= {"<100>"=<n> ...}] [direction= <list>] [doping= <list>] [fields.values= <list>] [fill.buried] [mask=<c>] [material=<c>] [polygon= <list>] [rate=<n>][<um/min>] [region.name=<c>] [remesh] [repair] [sde=<c>] [selective.materials= <list>] [shadowing] [sources= {<beam1> ...}] [steps=<n>] [Strained.Lattice] [suppress.remesh] [temperature=<n>][<C|K>] [time=<n>][<min|s>] [type= isotropic | anisotropic | ...]
deposit.description: Simulates a deposition step and deposits a new layer of the specified material onto the current structure. Supports isotropic, anisotropic, fill, Fourier, crystal, polygon, directional, and trapezoidal deposition modes. Can also deposit doped layers and handle shadowing effects.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material to deposit (e.g., Oxide, Silicon, Nitride).

parameters.name: type=
parameters.desc: Deposition mode: isotropic, anisotropic, crystal, directional, fill, fourier, polygon, or trapezoidal.

parameters.name: thickness=
parameters.desc: Thickness of the deposited layer. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: rate=
parameters.desc: Deposition rate (used with time). Default unit: um/min.

parameters.name: time=
parameters.desc: Deposition time (used with rate). Default unit: min.

parameters.name: coord=
parameters.desc: X-coordinate for type=fill. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: material=
parameters.desc: Material to deposit (overrides <material> positional arg).

parameters.name: direction=
parameters.desc: Direction vector for directional deposition {x y z}.

parameters.name: angle=
parameters.desc: Sidewall angle for trapezoidal deposition in degrees. Default: 90.

parameters.name: mask=
parameters.desc: Name of a mask to use for deposition.

parameters.name: region.name=
parameters.desc: Name for the newly created region (no underscore or period allowed).

parameters.name: doping=
parameters.desc: List of doping profile names for doped deposition.

parameters.name: fields.values=
parameters.desc: List of field-value pairs to initialize in the deposited layer (e.g., {Boron=1e18}).

parameters.name: selective.materials=
parameters.desc: List of materials where deposition will occur.

parameters.name: shadowing
parameters.desc: Enables shadowing effects for level-set or Fourier deposition.

parameters.name: sources=
parameters.desc: Defines source beams for level-set deposition.

parameters.name: coeffs=
parameters.desc: Fourier deposition coefficients A0, A1, ..., An.

parameters.name: sde=
parameters.desc: Arguments for 3D Sentaurus Structure Editor algorithms.

parameters.name: steps=
parameters.desc: Number of subdivision steps (for stress relaxation). Default: 1.

parameters.name: remesh
parameters.desc: Performs remeshing after deposition.

parameters.name: suppress.remesh
parameters.desc: Suppresses mesh generation before and after 3D deposition.

parameters.name: Strained.Lattice
parameters.desc: Specifies strained deposition.

parameters.name: temperature=
parameters.desc: Deposition temperature for stress relaxation. Default: 26.85C.

parameters.name: polygon=
parameters.desc: List of x/y coordinates for 2D polygon deposition.

parameters.name: 1D
parameters.desc: Prevents automatic dimension increase for polygon deposition.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing for deposition.

parameters.name: repair
parameters.desc: Enables small region removal in 3D MGOALS mode. Default: true.

deposit.example:
deposit thickness= 0.2 oxide isotropic
deposit rate= {0.2} material= {oxide} type= isotropic time= 1

deposit.intrinsic.description: Deposits an intrinsic (undoped) material layer. Used to deposit a layer without introducing any dopant species.

deposit.type.description: Specifies the deposition type for the current deposit command. Controls whether the deposition is anisotropic, isotropic, or uses a specific fill method.

parameters.name: type
parameters.desc: Deposition type: anisotropic, isotropic, or fill.

diffuse.description: Simulates thermal annealing, densification, and any material growth process during annealing including oxidation, silicidation, and epitaxy. Supports temperature ramps, multiple gas ambients, KMC diffusion, laser annealing, and LKMC epitaxial growth. If diffusion time is zero, it activates dopants and performs a stress update.

parameters.name: temp.ramp=
parameters.desc: Name of a temperature ramp created with the temp_ramp command.

parameters.name: time=
parameters.desc: Annealing time (default unit: minute).

parameters.name: temperature=
parameters.desc: Annealing temperature (default unit: degree Celsius).

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing for this diffusion step.

parameters.name: <ambient>
parameters.desc: Active ambient gas (O2, H2O, N2O, N2, H2, HCl, Cl2, Epi, LTE).

parameters.name: pressure=
parameters.desc: Total ambient gas pressure (default: 1.0 atm).

parameters.name: ramprate=
parameters.desc: Temperature change rate (default unit: K/s).

parameters.name: t.final=
parameters.desc: Final temperature for ramp (default unit: degree Celsius).

parameters.name: epi.model=
parameters.desc: Epitaxy method: 0 (moving-boundary, default) or 1 (alternating deposition/anneal).

parameters.name: epi.thickness=
parameters.desc: Epitaxial layer thickness to deposit (default unit: um).

parameters.name: epi.doping=
parameters.desc: Initial doping list for epitaxial layer, e.g. {boron=1e18<cm-3>}.

parameters.name: gas.flow=
parameters.desc: Name of a predefined gas_flow to use.

parameters.name: kmc
parameters.desc: Uses Sentaurus Process Kinetic Monte Carlo (KMC) for diffusion.

parameters.name: laser
parameters.desc: Switches on laser annealing mode.

parameters.name: lkmc
parameters.desc: Invokes lattice KMC model during epitaxial growth.

parameters.name: ISSG
parameters.desc: Switches on in situ steam-generated oxidation.

parameters.name: stress.relax
parameters.desc: Switches on stress relaxation during diffusion with inert ambient.

parameters.name: maxstep=
parameters.desc: Maximum time step (default unit: minute).

parameters.name: init=
parameters.desc: First time step (default unit: minute).

diffuse.example:
diffuse temperature= 1050<C> time= 10.0<s>
diffuse temperature= 850<C> time= 10.0<min> O2

doping.signature: doping field=<c> name=<c> (depths=<list> | times=<list>) values=<list> [clear] [list] [location= vertex | element] [log.grad] [stress.values=<list>]
doping.description: Defines a named piecewise linear doping profile that can be used with the deposit command. Also supports specifying stress field profiles for deposited layers. The profile is applied on vertices or elements at specified depths or times.

parameters.name: field=
parameters.desc: Name of the field (dopant, mole fraction, or stress component such as StressELXX).

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the profile, referenced in the deposit command.

parameters.name: depths=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of depths where values are applied (default unit: um).

parameters.name: times=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of times where values are applied (unit: minutes, for crystallographic/Fourier deposition only).

parameters.name: values=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of dopant field values (default unit: cm^-3).

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears all doping specifications.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns the names of all doping specifications.

parameters.name: location=
parameters.desc: Where to apply the field: vertex (default) or element.

parameters.name: log.grad
parameters.desc: Specifies a piecewise logarithmic doping profile.

parameters.name: stress.values=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of stress values (default unit: dyn/cm2).

doping.example:
doping name= init_boron field= Boron values= {1e10 1e20} depths= {0 0.1}
doping name= PolyDop field= Arsenic depths= {0 0.5} values= {1e20 1e20}

element.description: Extracts the grid for a specified material and returns it as a list of coordinates. Each outer list makes up a continuous line through the grid, with inner lists containing coordinate pairs. Not available for 3D simulations.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Name of the material to extract the grid from.

parameters.name: region
parameters.desc: Limits output to only one region if specified.

element.to.gauss.description: Enables the conversion of analytic implant profiles (Pearson or dual-Pearson) to Gaussian representations. This allows subsequent analytical operations on the implanted profiles.

equation.description: Test-parses and resolves an equation string. Useful for debugging equation resolver and parsing problems by trying equation strings before they are run in simulation.

parameters.name: eqn=
parameters.desc: Equation string to be checked and resolved.

parameters.name: nodal
parameters.desc: Returns only the nodal part of the string specified by eqn.

etch.signature: etch [<material>] [anisotropic | cmp | isotropic | trapezoidal] [angle=<n>] [coeffs= {<A0> <A1> ...}] [coord=<n>][<um>] [crystal.rate= {"<100>"=<n> ...}] [direction= <list>] [etchstop= {<mat1> ...}] [etchstop.overetch=<n>] [force.full.levelset] [mask=<c>] [material= <list>] [polygon= <list>] [rate= <list>] [remesh] [repair] [sde=<c>] [shadowing] [sources= {<beam1> ...}] [steps=<n>] [suppress.remesh] [thickness=<n>][<um>] [time=<n>][<min|s>] [type= anisotropic | cmp | cmp.flat | crystal | directional | fourier | isotropic | polygon | trapezoidal] [undercut=<n>]
etch.description: Removes part or all of an exposed layer. Supports multiple etching modes including isotropic, anisotropic, CMP, Fourier, crystal, directional, polygon, and trapezoidal. Can perform etch-stop on specified materials and handle shadowing effects.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material to etch.

parameters.name: material=
parameters.desc: String list of materials for multimaterial etching.

parameters.name: type=
parameters.desc: Etching mode: anisotropic, cmp, cmp.flat, crystal, directional, fourier, isotropic, polygon, or trapezoidal.

parameters.name: thickness=
parameters.desc: Thickness to remove. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: rate=
parameters.desc: Etching rate(s). Default unit: um/min.

parameters.name: time=
parameters.desc: Etching time (used with rate). Default unit: min.

parameters.name: direction=
parameters.desc: Direction vector for directional etching {x y z}.

parameters.name: angle=
parameters.desc: Etching angle for trapezoidal etching in degrees.

parameters.name: mask=
parameters.desc: Name of the mask to use for etching.

parameters.name: etchstop=
parameters.desc: List of materials that stop etching when exposed.

parameters.name: etchstop.overetch=
parameters.desc: Overetch fraction after etchstop. Default: 0.1 (10%).

parameters.name: coeffs=
parameters.desc: Fourier etching coefficients A0, A1, ..., An.

parameters.name: undercut=
parameters.desc: Underetch distance below nonetchable material (2D trapezoidal). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: shadowing
parameters.desc: Enables shadowing effects for level-set or Fourier etching.

parameters.name: sources=
parameters.desc: Defines etching source beams for level-set etching.

parameters.name: sde=
parameters.desc: Arguments for 3D Sentaurus Structure Editor algorithms.

parameters.name: remesh
parameters.desc: Performs remeshing after etching.

parameters.name: force.full.levelset
parameters.desc: Switches on general level-set time-stepping algorithm.

parameters.name: steps=
parameters.desc: Number of subdivision steps. Default: 1.

parameters.name: polygon=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of x/y coordinates for polygon etching.

etch.example: etch time= 2.0 rate= {0.1} material= {silicon} type= directional direction= {1.0 1.0 0.0} mask= m1

etch.rate.modifier.description: Specifies a multiplier or modification to the etch rate for specific materials. Used to fine-tune etch rates in complex etch processes.

parameters.name: rate.modifier
parameters.desc: Rate modifier expressions paired with material names.

extract.description: Specifies commands for extracting historical data during a diffuse step. Extracted data can be returned as a Tcl list, printed, or saved to a .plt file for visualization in Sentaurus Visual and Inspect.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the data extraction.

parameters.name: command=
parameters.desc: List of commands for data interpolation (select/interpolate pairs).

parameters.name: extract.variable.names=
parameters.desc: List of names for the output variables in the command list.

parameters.name: file.name=
parameters.desc: Name of the output .plt file for storing extracted variables.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears all stored historical data.

parameters.name: print
parameters.desc: Returns the extracted data values as a Tcl list.

parameters.name: syntax.check.value=
parameters.desc: Value to return only during syntax-checking mode.

extract.example: extract name= etest command= {select z= Boron interpolate Silicon x= 0.04}

extract.moments.description: Extracts the statistical moments (range, straggle, skewness, kurtosis) of implanted dopant profiles. Used for analyzing implant profile characteristics.

extract.variable.name.description: Specifies the name of a single variable to extract from the simulation results. The extracted value is stored for later reference.

parameters.name: variable.name
parameters.desc: Name of the variable to extract.

extract.variable.names.description: Specifies a list of variable names to extract from the simulation results. Used to retrieve multiple simulation quantities at once.

parameters.name: variable.names
parameters.desc: List of variable names to extract.

fproc.description: Alias for defineproc. Defines a Tcl procedure that is saved and restored in TDR files. Functionally identical to defineproc.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the Tcl procedure.

parameters.name: <procedure_arguments>
parameters.desc: List of arguments for the procedure.

parameters.name: <body_of_procedure>
parameters.desc: Body of the Tcl procedure.

fset.description: Defines a Tcl variable that is saved and restored from TDR files. Equivalent to the define command and the Tcl set command, except that variables defined with set are not persisted in TDR files. Commonly used to store ICVWB bounding box coordinates and other layout-driven parameters.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Any user-defined parameter name.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Any number or string value.

fset.example:
fset Ymin [icwb bbox left ]
fset SD_L 300
fset M1_left [expr {$SD_L + 25}]

gas_flow.description: Specifies a gas mixture for thermal oxidation or user-defined gas material reactions. The mixture is referenced by name in diffuse or temp_ramp commands. Supports flow-based or partial-pressure-based specification, ISSG oxidation, and anisotropic gas trajectories.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the gas mixture for reference in diffuse or temp_ramp.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears the global list of gas mixtures.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a Tcl list of defined gas mixture names.

parameters.name: print
parameters.desc: Prints the gas flow information.

parameters.name: <ambient>
parameters.desc: Shorthand to set partial pressure equal to total pressure (O2, H2O, N2O).

parameters.name: flow<ambient>=
parameters.desc: Gas flow rate for a specific ambient (default unit: l/min).

parameters.name: flows=
parameters.desc: List of gas flows, e.g. {O2= 3.1 H2O= 1.2 H2= 0.8}.

parameters.name: p<ambient>=
parameters.desc: Partial pressure of a specific ambient (default unit: atm).

parameters.name: partial.pressure=
parameters.desc: List of partial pressures, e.g. {O2= 1.0<atm> N2O= 1.0<atm>}.

parameters.name: pressure=
parameters.desc: Total ambient gas pressure (default: 1.0 atm).

parameters.name: ISSG
parameters.desc: Switches on in situ steam-generated oxidation.

parameters.name: aniso.exponent=
parameters.desc: Angular distribution exponent for anisotropic gas trajectories per ambient (default: 1).

gas_flow.example: gas_flow name= myflow pressure=0.8 flows= {O2= 3.1 H2O= 1.2 H2= 0.8}

graphics.description: Controls plot settings in Sentaurus Visual and selects which fields are available for visualization. Configures display regions, field ranges, color scaling, and axis limits for both interactive and batch plotting.

parameters.name: add.active
parameters.desc: Includes active dopant concentrations and NetActive in 1D plots. Use !add.active to disable. Default: true.

parameters.name: add.dopants
parameters.desc: Includes dopant fields in plots. Use !add.dopants to disable. Default: true.

parameters.name: add.total
parameters.desc: Includes total concentration fields in plots. Use !add.total to disable. Default: true.

parameters.name: cmd=
parameters.desc: Tcl script executed on each graphics update in addition to the default update.

parameters.name: display.all=
parameters.desc: Switches on/off all bulk, field, mesh, and borders for listed regions/materials. Use !name to hide.

parameters.name: display.border=
parameters.desc: Controls visibility of region boundaries. Takes material/region names or all.

parameters.name: display.bulk=
parameters.desc: Controls visibility of material fills. Takes material/region names or all.

parameters.name: display.field=
parameters.desc: Controls visibility of field data. Takes material/region names or all.

parameters.name: display.mesh=
parameters.desc: Controls visibility of mesh. Takes material/region names or all.

parameters.name: field.ld.max=
parameters.desc: Maximum of y-axis for xy plots (1D).

parameters.name: field.ld.min=
parameters.desc: Minimum of y-axis for xy plots (1D).

parameters.name: field.max=
parameters.desc: Maximum color contour values for 2D/3D field display. Format: {Boron=1e20}.

parameters.name: field.min=
parameters.desc: Minimum color contour values for 2D/3D field display. Format: {Boron=1e10}.

parameters.name: field.scale=
parameters.desc: Scaling mode for contours (2D/3D) or y-axis (1D). Values: asinh, linear, log.

parameters.name: fields=
parameters.desc: List of fields to display. In 2D/3D, only the first is displayed; others are sent to Sentaurus Visual.

parameters.name: keep.aspect.ratio
parameters.desc: Maintains x/y aspect ratio (2D). Use !keep.aspect.ratio to allow independent axes. Default: true.

parameters.name: off
parameters.desc: Switches off automatic graphics updating.

parameters.name: on
parameters.desc: Switches on automatic graphics updating.

parameters.name: svisual.cmd=
parameters.desc: Tcl command sent directly to Sentaurus Visual for advanced control.

parameters.name: update
parameters.desc: Forces a graphics update immediately.

parameters.name: xmax=
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate of the plot range. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: xmin=
parameters.desc: Minimum x-coordinate of the plot range. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: ymax=
parameters.desc: Maximum y-coordinate of the plot range (2D only). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: ymin=
parameters.desc: Minimum y-coordinate of the plot range (2D only). Default unit: um.

grid.description: Sets meshing parameters, performs grid operations (remesh, merge, rename, extrude), and computes statistics about the mesh and boundary representation (brep).

parameters.name: remesh
parameters.desc: Recreates the mesh using the active mesh generator.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing during remesh.

parameters.name: merge
parameters.desc: Merges adjacent regions of the same material into one region. Cannot be combined with other arguments.

parameters.name: rename
parameters.desc: Renames all regions by material and y-coordinate position.

parameters.name: print.names
parameters.desc: Prints the region names when rename is specified.

parameters.name: interpolate
parameters.desc: Performs interpolation during remesh.

parameters.name: 2D | 3D | FullD
parameters.desc: Extrudes grid to higher dimension (2D, 3D, or highest possible).

parameters.name: get.refine.check
parameters.desc: Returns 1 if remeshing is needed based on refinement criteria, otherwise 0.

parameters.name: save.min.edge
parameters.desc: Computes the smallest edge length in each direction and saves to fields. Must be used by itself.

parameters.name: save.volume
parameters.desc: Sets element volumes as element values over the mesh.

parameters.name: sano.remesh
parameters.desc: Switches on Sano remesh mode for converting KMC particles to finite-element fields.

parameters.name: sano.smooth
parameters.desc: Converts KMC particle distributions to finite-element fields using the Sano method.

parameters.name: smooth.brep
parameters.desc: Smooths the boundary representation by removing noise and sharp features.

parameters.name: Gas
parameters.desc: Includes gas mesh in quality and bbox measures (default). Use !Gas to exclude.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Limits the measured grid statistics to the specified material.

parameters.name: set.Delaunay.type=
parameters.desc: Type of mesh generated: boxmethod, conformal (default), or constrained.

parameters.name: set.max.connectivity=
parameters.desc: Maximum number of elements connected to a point. Default: 1e37.

parameters.name: set.max.neighbor.ratio=
parameters.desc: Ratio of neighboring collinear edges in the binary tree for smoothing. Default: 3.0.

parameters.name: set.max.points=
parameters.desc: Maximum number of points allowed by the delaunization module. Default: 500000.

parameters.name: set.min.angle=
parameters.desc: Minimum angle allowed in mesh elements (2D only). Default: 0.

parameters.name: set.min.edge=
parameters.desc: Minimum edge length. Default: 2.0e-6 um.

parameters.name: set.min.grid=
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed grid spacing. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: set.min.normal.size=
parameters.desc: Smallest normal mesh element size at interfaces. Default: 8.0e-4 um.

parameters.name: set.normal.growth.ratio.2d=
parameters.desc: Element growth ratio normal from interface in 2D. Default: 2.0.

parameters.name: set.normal.growth.ratio.3d=
parameters.desc: Element growth ratio normal from interface in 3D. Default: 3.0.

parameters.name: set.sano.list=
parameters.desc: Fields for sano.remesh or sano.smooth operations. Default: active dopant concentrations.

parameters.name: set.sano.materials=
parameters.desc: Materials where the Sano method is applied. Default: Silicon.

parameters.name: set.delpsc.accuracy=
parameters.desc: Deviation between DelPSC surface and MLS surface. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: set.delpsc.resolution=
parameters.desc: Size of small triangles in the DelPSC algorithm. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: set.repair.resolution=
parameters.desc: Level-set cell size for MLS noise removal. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: get.mesh.stats
parameters.desc: Prints all bulk mesh statistics.

parameters.name: get.bbox | get.bbox.cm | get.bbox.um
parameters.desc: Prints the bounding box of the structure.

parameters.name: get.elements
parameters.desc: Returns the number of elements.

parameters.name: get.nodes
parameters.desc: Returns the number of bulk nodes.

parameters.name: get.total.nodes
parameters.desc: Returns the total number of nodes (including interface nodes).

parameters.name: get.vertices
parameters.desc: Returns the total number of vertices.

parameters.name: get.brep.stats
parameters.desc: Prints all boundary representation statistics.

help.description: Prints a list of all commands available in Sentaurus Process. Can be used in both interactive mode and command files.

icwb.description: Controls the IC Workbench (ICVWB) layout-driven simulation interface. Provides commands for reading GDSII or ICWB layout files, querying domain information, managing simulation domains, listing layers and polygons, and applying stretch transformations. Supports both 2D and 3D simulations.

parameters.name: gds.file=
parameters.desc: Path to the GDSII layout file to load.

parameters.name: cell=
parameters.desc: Cell name from the GDS file (? for first root cell).

parameters.name: sim2d=
parameters.desc: 2D simulation domain defined as {xmin ymin xmax ymax} in GDS coordinates.

parameters.name: sim3d=
parameters.desc: 3D simulation domain defined as {xmin ymin xmax ymax} in GDS coordinates.

parameters.name: layer.numbers=
parameters.desc: List of GDS layer numbers to select.

parameters.name: layer.names=
parameters.desc: List of names for the selected layers.

parameters.name: layer.map.file=
parameters.desc: File containing layer name to number mappings.

parameters.name: domain.name=
parameters.desc: Name for the simulation domain (default: SIM2D or SIM3D).

parameters.name: scale=
parameters.desc: Scale factor applied to GDS coordinates when reading.

parameters.name: !recenter
parameters.desc: Disables translation of GDS coordinates to (0,0) origin.

parameters.name: stretches=
parameters.desc: List of stretch definitions as {name={x1 y1 x2 y2}}.

parameters.name: filename=
parameters.desc: Path to an ICWB .mac layout file.

parameters.name: domain=
parameters.desc: Sets the current domain name.

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.desc: Returns bounding box coordinates (xmin|xmax|ymin|ymax|left|right|front|back).

parameters.name: dimension
parameters.desc: Returns the dimension of the current domain (1=Point, 2=Gauge, 3=Highlight).

parameters.name: stretch name= value=
parameters.desc: Applies a named stretch by the given amount.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Lists domains, layers, polygons, labels, or bounding boxes.

icwb.example: icwb gds.file= layout.gds cell= Inverter sim2d= "$xmin $ymin $xmax $ymax" scale= 1e-3 layer.numbers= {1:0 2:0 3:0} layer.names= {NWELL NPDIFF POLY}

icwb.composite.signature: icwb.composite cell=<c> gds.file=<c> composite.domain.name=<list> sim2d.list=<list> layer.names=<list> layer.numbers=<list> ?scale=<n>? ?stretches=<list>?
icwb.composite.description: Loads a GDSII file for a 2D TCAD simulation based on composite simulation domains. Similar to the icwb gds.file command but applies to 2D composite domains formed by joining multiple regular 2D simulation domains.

parameters.name: gds.file=
parameters.desc: Name of the input GDSII file.

parameters.name: cell=
parameters.desc: Cell name in the GDS file.

parameters.name: composite.domain.name=
parameters.desc: Name of the composite domain; multiple subdomain names joined by plus signs (e.g., BJT1+BJT2).

parameters.name: sim2d.list=
parameters.desc: Sequence of 2D simulation domains as {{x1 y1 x2 y2} {x1 y1 x2 y2} ...}.

parameters.name: layer.names=
parameters.desc: List of names for the selected layers.

parameters.name: layer.numbers=
parameters.desc: List of selected layer numbers from the GDS file.

parameters.name: scale=
parameters.desc: Scale factor for GDS coordinates.

parameters.name: stretches=
parameters.desc: List of stretches as {name={x1 y1 x2 y2}}.

icwb.composite.example: icwb.composite gds.file= BCD.gds cell= Inverter scale= 1e-3 layer.numbers= {1:0 2:0 3:0} layer.names= {NWELL NACTIVE PACTIVE} composite.domain.name= "BJT1+BJT2" sim2d.list= {{6200.0 8150.0 8400.0 8150.0} {8250.0 8800.0 8250.0 9900.0}}

icwb.contact.mask.description: Creates contacts for subsequent device simulations tied to a layer or text label in the ICWB layout file. Supports both box-type contacts (surface elements within a box) and point-type contacts (all boundary elements of a region). The lateral placement is obtained from the ICWB layer or label, while the vertical placement is from contact command arguments.

parameters.name: label.name=
parameters.desc: Name of a text label in the ICWB layout file.

parameters.name: layer.name=
parameters.desc: Name of a layer in the ICWB layout file.

parameters.name: distance=
parameters.desc: Distance from the 2D simulation domain for label recognition (default: 0.03 um).

parameters.name: label.in.polygon=
parameters.desc: If 1, only polygons containing the label are used (default: 0).

parameters.name: box
parameters.desc: Creates a box-type contact.

parameters.name: point
parameters.desc: Creates a point-type contact.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material for the contact.

parameters.name: adjacent.material=
parameters.desc: Adjacent material for box-type contact.

parameters.name: boxheight=
parameters.desc: Vertical extent of the box contact (default: 0.1 um).

parameters.name: xlo=
parameters.desc: Lower x-coordinate of the box contact.

parameters.name: xhi=
parameters.desc: Upper x-coordinate of the box contact.

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Replaces the original material with gas in the TDR file (point type only).

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: Height coordinate for point-type contact.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the contact. Defaults to label name or layer name.

icwb.contact.mask.example: icwb.contact.mask layer.name= emitter box polysilicon adjacent.material= oxide xlo= -2.05 xhi= -1.95

icwb.create.all.masks.description: Creates positive and negative mask versions for all layers found in the currently active ICWB simulation domain. Mask names are derived from layer names with suffix _p (positive) and _n (negative). Supports reflect, shift, stretch, and save-to-TDR operations for each mask.

parameters.name: reflecty=
parameters.desc: Reflects the layer around the y-axis at the given coordinate.

parameters.name: reflectz=
parameters.desc: Reflects the layer around the z-axis at the given coordinate.

parameters.name: save.to.tdr=
parameters.desc: Whether to save masks to TDR file (default: 200 point limit).

parameters.name: shift=
parameters.desc: Shift amount as {dy dz} before creating masks.

parameters.name: stretchyneg=
parameters.desc: Stretch layer to the negative y-side of the position as {yo dy}.

parameters.name: stretchypos=
parameters.desc: Stretch layer to the positive y-side of the position as {yo dy}.

parameters.name: stretchzneg=
parameters.desc: Stretch layer to the negative z-side of the position as {zo dz}.

parameters.name: stretchzpos=
parameters.desc: Stretch layer to the positive z-side of the position as {zo dz}.

icwb.create.mask.description: Creates a mask from one or more ICWB layers for use in deposit, etch, or photo commands. Automatically determines the dimension of the active ICWB domain. If multiple layer names are given, the resulting mask is the union of all layer polygons (3D) or segments (2D). Supports shift, stretch, and reflect operations.

parameters.name: layer.name=
parameters.desc: Name of one or more ICWB layers (space-separated list for multiple).

parameters.name: exit.if.not.found=
parameters.desc: If 1, simulation exits if a layer is not found; otherwise continues with a warning (default: 0).

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the mask (defaults to layer name).

parameters.name: polarity=
parameters.desc: Mask polarity: positive (default) or negative.

parameters.name: reflecty=
parameters.desc: Reflects the layer around the y-axis at the given coordinate.

parameters.name: reflectz=
parameters.desc: Reflects the layer around the z-axis at the given coordinate.

parameters.name: save.to.tdr=
parameters.desc: Whether to save the mask to TDR (0=no, 1=yes with point limit).

parameters.name: shift=
parameters.desc: Shift amount as {dy dz} before creating the mask.

parameters.name: stretchyneg=
parameters.desc: Stretch to negative y-side as {yo dy}.

parameters.name: stretchypos=
parameters.desc: Stretch to positive y-side as {yo dy}.

parameters.name: stretchzneg=
parameters.desc: Stretch to negative z-side as {zo dz}.

parameters.name: stretchzpos=
parameters.desc: Stretch to positive z-side as {zo dz}.

icwb.create.mask.example:
icwb.create.mask layer.name= "NPDIFF PPDIFF NPLUG PBASE" name= STI_n
icwb.create.mask layer.name= NWELL polarity= negative

implant.description: Specifies implantation model parameters and implants an ion species into a wafer. Supports analytic functions (Gaussian, Pearson, dual-Pearson) and Monte Carlo methods (Sentaurus MC, KMC). The command has two branches: model specification and actual implantation execution.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Dopant species name (e.g., Boron, Arsenic, Phosphorus, Antimony, Germanium).

parameters.name: dose=
parameters.desc: Implant dose (default unit: cm^-2).

parameters.name: energy=
parameters.desc: Implant energy (default unit: keV).

parameters.name: tilt=
parameters.desc: Tilt angle in degrees (default: 7).

parameters.name: rotation=
parameters.desc: Rotation angle in degrees (default: -90).

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing after implantation.

parameters.name: beam.dose
parameters.desc: Overrides dose control to use beam dose mode.

parameters.name: sentaurus.mc
parameters.desc: Uses the Sentaurus Monte Carlo implantation method.

parameters.name: KMC
parameters.desc: Uses the Kinetic Monte Carlo method for implantation.

parameters.name: plasma
parameters.desc: Switches on plasma implantation mode.

parameters.name: damage
parameters.desc: Switches on damage calculation based on the Hobler model.

parameters.name: mult.rot=
parameters.desc: Number of multirotation implantations with a revolving ion beam.

parameters.name: temperature=
parameters.desc: Wafer temperature during implantation (default unit: degree Celsius).

implant.example:
implant Boron dose= 2e13<cm-2> energy= 200<keV> tilt= 0 rotation= 0
implant Arsenic dose= 4e14<cm-2> energy= 10<keV> tilt= 0 rotation= 0

init.description: Sets up the mesh from either a rectangular specification or a file, and initializes background doping concentration. Also configures wafer orientation, slice angle, and miscut for implantation coordinate systems.

parameters.name: concentration=
parameters.desc: Concentration of the data field (default unit: cm^-3).

parameters.name: field=
parameters.desc: Name of the data field to initialize everywhere.

parameters.name: fields.values=
parameters.desc: List of field-value pairs, e.g. {Boron=1e18<cm-3>}.

parameters.name: tdr=
parameters.desc: Prefix of a TDR format file to read for geometry and data restart.

parameters.name: wafer.orient=
parameters.desc: Wafer orientation as three Miller indices (default: {0 0 1}).

parameters.name: slice.angle=
parameters.desc: Angle of simulation domain vs. wafer coordinate system (default: -90 degrees).

parameters.name: notch.direction=
parameters.desc: Direction from wafer center to notch as Miller indices (default: {1 1 0}).

parameters.name: miscut.tilt=
parameters.desc: Wafer miscut angle in degrees (default: 0).

parameters.name: miscut.toward=
parameters.desc: Miscut orientation as three Miller indices.

parameters.name: species=
parameters.desc: Data field names to read from TDR file. Empty list reads none.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Uses adaptive meshing when loading TDR geometry without mesh.

parameters.name: pdb
parameters.desc: Loads PDB parameters from TDR file (default: true).

parameters.name: top
parameters.desc: Places gas at smallest x-value (default). !top places it at largest.

insert.description: Inserts segments into 1D structures, polygons into 2D structures, and polyhedra into 3D structures. Can also perform polyhedron/polygon etching (with new.material=Gas) or deposition (with replace.materials=Gas).

parameters.name: polygon=
parameters.desc: Name of a previously defined polygon to insert (2D).

parameters.name: polyhedron=
parameters.desc: Name of a previously defined polyhedron to insert (3D).

parameters.name: segments=
parameters.desc: List of coordinates defining 1D segments (in um).

parameters.name: new.material=
parameters.desc: Material for the inserted geometry (mandatory except for TDR polyhedra).

parameters.name: new.region=
parameters.desc: Name for the inserted region.

parameters.name: replace.materials=
parameters.desc: Materials to be replaced by the polyhedron. Use bulk.materials for all nongas.

parameters.name: replace.regions=
parameters.desc: Regions to be replaced by the polyhedron.

parameters.name: accuracy=
parameters.desc: Snapping accuracy relative to bounding box. Range: (0, 1e-2]. Default: 1e-7.

parameters.name: repair
parameters.desc: Enables repair of geometric artifacts from polyhedron insertion. Default: true.

parameters.name: fields.values=
parameters.desc: List of field-value pairs for the inserted geometry.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing if remeshing.

insert.example:
insert polyhedron= prism new.material= Gas
insert polyhedron= smallCube new.material= Oxide

integrate.description: Returns the volume integration of a named quantity over the entire structure or within a specified box. Returns a Tcl list with the integrated value, volume, dose, minimum, maximum, and optionally the average.

parameters.name: absolute
parameters.desc: Integrates with absolute values of the named quantity.

parameters.name: average
parameters.desc: Computes and appends the average value to the returned list.

parameters.name: element
parameters.desc: Integrates elementwise (expects elemental quantity).

parameters.name: interfaces
parameters.desc: Includes interface meshes in the integration.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Limits integration to regions of the specified material.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: Lower-right front corner of the integration box.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: Upper-left back corner of the integration box.

parameters.name: mode=
parameters.desc: Integration mode: mesh (default), boundary (3D only), or jagged.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Quantity to integrate (default: Z_Plot_Var).

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Limits integration to the named region.

parameters.name: skipgas
parameters.desc: Omits integration on invisible (gas) meshes.

integrate.example:
integrate silicon name= Boron
integrate name= Boron average min= {0. 0.} max= {10. 0.2}

interface.description: Returns the position of a material interface or the value of the selected data field at an interface. Can also extract the thickness of a region. Used to prepare plots of material thickness, silicon consumption, or material growth.

parameters.name: All
parameters.desc: Returns all interface locations instead of only the first.

parameters.name: data
parameters.desc: Returns the value of the selected data field at the interface.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material or interface description (e.g., silicon /oxide).

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the data field to return when data is specified (default: Z_Plot_Var).

parameters.name: p1=
parameters.desc: First endpoint of a cutline as {x y z}.

parameters.name: p2=
parameters.desc: Second endpoint of a cutline as {x y z}.

parameters.name: precision=
parameters.desc: Number of precision digits for floating values (default: 6).

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Region name when using the thickness option.

parameters.name: side=
parameters.desc: Bulk material side for data value lookup.

parameters.name: thickness
parameters.desc: Extracts the thickness of a material or region (returned in um).

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: Lateral position for 2D/3D interface lookup (default unit: um).

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Vertical position for 2D/3D interface lookup (default unit: um).

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Depth position for 3D interface lookup (default unit: um).

interface.example:
interface y= 1.0 silicon /oxide
interface oxide /gas
interface thickness region= GateOxide

interface.mat.pairs.description: Specifies pairs of materials at whose interface recombination or other interface properties are applied. Each pair consists of two material names.

parameters.name: mat.pairs
parameters.desc: List of material name pairs for interface property assignment.

interface.materials.description: Specifies the materials at which interface properties or boundary conditions are applied. Used to define interface models between specific material pairs.

parameters.name: materials
parameters.desc: List of material names for interface definition.

interface.region.pairs.description: Specifies pairs of regions at whose interface properties are applied. Similar to mat.pairs but uses region names instead of material names for finer control.

parameters.name: region.pairs
parameters.desc: List of region name pairs for interface property assignment.

interface.regions.description: Specifies the regions at whose boundaries interface properties are applied. Used for region-level interface model assignment.

parameters.name: regions
parameters.desc: List of region names for interface definition.

interpolate.description: Returns the interpolated value of a data field at a specified position, or returns the position at which a specified data value occurs. In 1D, supply either x or value; in 2D, two of four arguments; in 3D, three of four. Returns a Tcl list if multiple matches are found.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Limits the search to a single material.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of a data field (default: Z_Plot_Var).

parameters.name: value=
parameters.desc: Data value to search for; returns positions where the field crosses this value.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: X-coordinate for value lookup (default unit: um).

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate for value lookup (default unit: um).

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Z-coordinate for value lookup (default unit: um).

parameters.name: max
parameters.desc: Reports the maximum of the specified field.

parameters.name: min
parameters.desc: Reports the minimum of the specified field.

parameters.name: report.location
parameters.desc: Reports coordinates where max/min occurs.

parameters.name: extract.variable.names=
parameters.desc: Custom names for output variables.

interpolate.example:
interpolate oxide x= 1.0 y= 1.0
interpolate y= 0.0 silicon value= 0.0
interpolate x= 2.0 silicon value= 0.0

kmc.description: Specifies options for the atomistic kinetic Monte Carlo (KMC) mode. Main subcommands include add (create defects), deatomize (convert particles to fields), extract (retrieve concentrations and defect information), and control of TDR file output.

parameters.name: add
parameters.desc: Adds defects to the KMC simulation queue; use kmc add to transfer from queue to simulation.

parameters.name: add queue
parameters.desc: Adds a defect to the queue at specified coordinates (coordx, coordy, coordz) with given name.

parameters.name: deatomize
parameters.desc: Builds a data field filled with KMC concentrations for a given species name.

parameters.name: extract
parameters.desc: Retrieves information about the current KMC simulation status.

parameters.name: extract profile
parameters.desc: Returns concentrations of particles, defects, electrons, or holes along cutlines.

parameters.name: extract defects
parameters.desc: Returns current list of particles/defects in the simulation.

parameters.name: extract dose
parameters.desc: Returns doses (concentration in cm-2) of particles or defects.

parameters.name: extract histogram
parameters.desc: Returns number of defects for each defect size.

parameters.name: extract supersaturation
parameters.desc: Returns interstitial or vacancy supersaturation ratio.

parameters.name: extract tdrAdd
parameters.desc: Adds a snapshot of KMC information to be written to a TDR file.

parameters.name: extract tdrClear
parameters.desc: Removes all previously stored TDR snapshots from memory.

parameters.name: extract tdrWrite
parameters.desc: Writes all stored TDR snapshots to a file.

parameters.name: extract materials
parameters.desc: Returns the list of materials currently in the simulation.

parameters.name: extract acinterface
parameters.desc: Returns 1D coordinates of amorphous-crystalline transitions.

parameters.name: extract epi.surface
parameters.desc: Returns the average x-value of the epitaxial surface.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the field, particle, or defect for the subcommand.

parameters.name: defectname=
parameters.desc: Refines name to a specific defect type (e.g., PointDefect, ImpurityCluster, ThreeOneOne).

parameters.name: coordx= | coordy= | coordz=
parameters.desc: Coordinates for add queue or cutline specification. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: materialname=
parameters.desc: Restricts output to the specified material.

parameters.name: countparticles
parameters.desc: Counts and returns the number of particles instead of listing them.

parameters.name: countdefects
parameters.desc: Counts the number of defects instead of listing particles.

parameters.name: meansize
parameters.desc: Returns average defect size instead of a histogram.

parameters.name: minsize=
parameters.desc: Minimum size for computing average cluster size.

parameters.name: timeaveraged
parameters.desc: Generates time-averaged concentrations of mobile particles.

parameters.name: remesh
parameters.desc: Remeshes the KMC structure, especially useful after etch/strip steps.

parameters.name: report
parameters.desc: Generates a report of defects created during the simulation.

parameters.name: off
parameters.desc: Deletes KMC information and removes the KMC object from memory.

parameters.name: defecttypes
parameters.desc: Returns the names of modeled defect types.

parameters.name: particletypes
parameters.desc: Returns the list of valid particle names.

parameters.name: materialtypes
parameters.desc: Returns the subset of materials allowed in KMC simulation.

parameters.name: present
parameters.desc: Returns true (1) if the species in name is present in the KMC simulation.

parameters.name: PDEupdated
parameters.desc: Returns true if KMC state has not changed since the last PDE synchronization.

parameters.name: clustertype
parameters.desc: Returns the type of a defect (amorphous pocket, {311}, or loop).

parameters.name: force.remesh
parameters.desc: Forces creation of a new finite-element structure from stored LKMC contour information (3D only).

parameters.name: active
parameters.desc: Deatomizes only the active part of a dopant.

kmc.example: set BTotal   [kmc extract profile name= Boron ]

kmc.reset.snapshot.description: Resets the Kinetic Monte Carlo (KMC) snapshot state. Used to clear the stored KMC state and restart the KMC simulation from the current configuration.

kmc.stress.description: Enables or queries stress calculation within the Kinetic Monte Carlo simulation. When enabled, KMC accounts for stress effects on defect diffusion and reaction rates.

layers.description: Prints the material interfaces and integrates the selected data field in each region. Most useful for examining doping distributions across layers.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Limits reporting to regions of the specified material.

parameters.name: merge
parameters.desc: Merges adjacent regions with the same material. Default: false.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of a data field to integrate. Default: Z_Plot_Var.

parameters.name: p1=
parameters.desc: Start point {x y z} for an arbitrary cutline section.
