parameters.name: p2=
parameters.desc: End point {x y z} for an arbitrary cutline section.

parameters.name: precision=
parameters.desc: Number of precision digits for floating values. Default: 12.

parameters.name: region.names
parameters.desc: Prints region names in addition to material names.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: X-coordinate for constant-plane sectioning. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate for constant-plane sectioning. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Z-coordinate for constant-plane sectioning. Default unit: um.

layers.example:
layers
layers y= 0.0 name= Boron
layers p1= {-1 0 0} p2= {4 2 0} region.names

line.signature: line location=<n>[<um>] (x | y | z) [clear] [kmc] [mgoals] [spacing=<n>][<um>] [spacing.method= even | regular | smooth] [tag=<c>] [virtual.spacing]
line.description: Specifies the position and spacing of mesh lines. Used with the init and region commands to create the initial mesh for rectilinear structures. Supports virtual spacing for soft mesh refinement guidance.

parameters.name: location=
parameters.desc: Position along the chosen axis. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: x | y | z
parameters.desc: Orientation of the mesh line (constant x, y, or z plane).

parameters.name: spacing=
parameters.desc: Local grid spacing at this location. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: spacing.method=
parameters.desc: Tick distribution method: even, regular, or smooth. Default: smooth.

parameters.name: tag=
parameters.desc: Label for later reference by region commands.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears all lines and mesh ticks for a new structure definition.

parameters.name: kmc
parameters.desc: Controls whether line applies to KMC mesh. Default: true.

parameters.name: mgoals
parameters.desc: Controls whether line applies to continuum mesh. Default: true.

parameters.name: virtual.spacing
parameters.desc: Creates virtual (soft) ticks between lines for refinement guidance.

line.example:
line x location= 0 spacing= 0.02 tag= surf
line y location= 0 spacing= 1 tag= left
line y location= 2 spacing= 1 tag= right

line_edge_roughness.description: Adds line edge roughness (LER) to specified masks by applying randomized deviations to mask edges. Controls LER characteristics through correlation length, standard deviation, segment length, and optional corner smoothing.

parameters.name: masks=
parameters.desc: List of mask names to receive LER.

parameters.name: normal=
parameters.desc: Defines the normal axis ("Y" or "Z"); only mask segments normal to this axis receive LER.

parameters.name: standard.deviation=
parameters.desc: Standard deviation of the randomized LER (noise amplitude). Default: 2.0 um.

parameters.name: correlation.length=
parameters.desc: Correlation length (wavelength) of the LER. Default: 20.0 um.

parameters.name: max.segment.length=
parameters.desc: Maximum segment length for mask edge subdivision before LER is applied. Default: 1.0 um.

parameters.name: max.tries=
parameters.desc: Maximum number of LER generation attempts with decimation detection. Default: 0.

parameters.name: min.radius=
parameters.desc: Corner rounding radius when normal is not specified. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: random.reseed | !random.reseed
parameters.desc: Reseeds random number generator before LER calculation. Default: true.

parameters.name: random.seed=
parameters.desc: Fixed random seed for reproducible LER across runs.

parameters.name: smooth.points=
parameters.desc: Number of boundary segments to smooth for mirror boundary conditions. Default: 0.

line_edge_roughness.example: line_edge_roughness normal= "Z" masks= {mask1} correlation.length= 25<nm> standard.deviation= 5<nm> max.segment.length= 5<nm>

load.signature: load (tdr=<c> (merge | rename | replace | sum) ?fast.tdr.ave=<list>? ?flip (left|right|front|back|up|down)? ?keep.outside? ?material.specific? ?offset={<n> <n>}> ?save.transform=<c>? ?shift=<n>? ?species=<list> multiply=<list> actions=<list>? ?transform={<n> ...}>? | commands=<list>)
load.description: Interpolates data from a TDR file onto the current mesh. Supports multiple loading modes: merge (new datasets only), rename (append __load suffix), replace (overwrite existing), or sum (add to existing). Can flip, shift, transform, or offset the loaded data before interpolation. Also supports distributed loading of multiple TDR files and fast averaging of identical meshes.

parameters.name: tdr=
parameters.desc: Name of the TDR file to load.

parameters.name: merge
parameters.desc: Adds only new datasets that do not currently exist.

parameters.name: rename
parameters.desc: Adds new datasets and renames by appending __load.

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Adds new datasets and replaces existing ones with the same name.

parameters.name: sum
parameters.desc: Adds new datasets and sums matching datasets.

parameters.name: fast.tdr.ave=
parameters.desc: Averages data from a list of TDR files with identical meshes.

parameters.name: flip
parameters.desc: Flips data in the specified direction before interpolation (left|right|front|back|up|down).

parameters.name: keep.outside
parameters.desc: Fields outside the loaded TDR bounding box remain unchanged (default: false).

parameters.name: !material.specific
parameters.desc: Allows interpolation regardless of material match.

parameters.name: offset=
parameters.desc: 2D only: offsets data by a vector before loading.

parameters.name: shift=
parameters.desc: Shifts data laterally before loading.

parameters.name: save.transform=
parameters.desc: Saves a TDR file of the transformed structure for verification.

parameters.name: species=
parameters.desc: List of specific field names to load from the TDR file.

parameters.name: multiply=
parameters.desc: Multiplication coefficients for each species.

parameters.name: actions=
parameters.desc: Per-species actions: merge, rename, replace, or sum.

parameters.name: transform=
parameters.desc: Transformation matrix and offset for rotation/translation.

parameters.name: commands=
parameters.desc: List of load commands for distributed loading of multiple TDR files.

load.commands.description: Specifies a file containing Tcl commands to be loaded and executed. Used to include external command scripts into the current simulation flow.

parameters.name: commands
parameters.desc: Path to the Tcl command file to load.

load.mc.description: Loads Monte Carlo implantation tables or data. Used to initialize Monte Carlo simulation parameters from pre-computed data files.

mask.signature: mask ?[bbox|bbox.cm|bbox.um]? ?bool=<c>? ?clear? ?covered.status? ?cut.x=<n> (materials=<list>|regions=<list>)? ?get.segments? ?list? ?name=<c>? ?negative? ?positive? ?(polygons=<list> | segments=<list> | left=<n> right=<n> front=<n> back=<n>)?
mask.description: Manages and creates masks for use with subsequent deposit, etch, or photo commands. Masks are defined by rectangles, polygons, and segments, or read from ICWB layout files. Masks are created additively (union of multiple calls with the same name). Supports Boolean operations, coverage queries, and segment retrieval.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the mask.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears all masks, or only the specified mask if name is given.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Prints a list of all defined masks (or details if name is given).

parameters.name: left=
parameters.desc: Left boundary of a rectangular mask (default unit: um).

parameters.name: right=
parameters.desc: Right boundary of a rectangular mask.

parameters.name: front=
parameters.desc: Front boundary of a rectangular mask.

parameters.name: back=
parameters.desc: Back boundary of a rectangular mask.

parameters.name: polygons=
parameters.desc: List of named polygons defined by polygon commands.

parameters.name: segments=
parameters.desc: List of mask segment coordinates.

parameters.name: negative
parameters.desc: Inverts the mask type (points inside are unmasked).

parameters.name: positive
parameters.desc: Sets the mask type to positive (default).

parameters.name: bool=
parameters.desc: Performs Boolean operations between masks.

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.desc: Returns the mask bounding box.

parameters.name: covered.status
parameters.desc: Returns coverage status: covered, uncovered, partial.2d, or partial.

parameters.name: cut.x=
parameters.desc: 2D only: x-coordinate for creating a mask from a material/region cross-section.

parameters.name: materials=
parameters.desc: Materials for cut.x mask creation.

parameters.name: regions=
parameters.desc: Regions for cut.x mask creation.

parameters.name: get.segments
parameters.desc: Retrieves segments from cutting the mask.

mask.example:
mask name= gate_mask left= -1 right= 90<nm>
mask name= field left= 0.0 right= 10.0
mask name= STI left= -$sti_width right= $sti_width front= -$sd_len back= $sd_len

mask.corner.mns.description: Specifies the material names for mask corner processing on the minimum-norm side. Controls how mask corners are handled during mesh refinement.

parameters.name: corner.mns
parameters.desc: List of material names for minimum-norm side corner processing.

mask.corner.ngr.description: Specifies the material names for mask corner processing on the no-growth-region side. Controls refinement behavior at mask corners.

parameters.name: corner.ngr
parameters.desc: List of material names for no-growth-region side corner processing.

mask.corner.refine.extent.description: Specifies the extent of refinement around mask corners. Controls how far from the corner the mesh refinement extends.

parameters.name: corner.refine.extent
parameters.desc: Refinement extent distance (default unit: um).

mask.discretization.size.description: Specifies the discretization size for mask edges. Controls the segment length used when converting mask edges into discrete segments for mesh generation.

parameters.name: discretization.size
parameters.desc: Discretization segment size (default unit: um).

mask.edge.mns.description: Specifies the material names for mask edge processing on the minimum-norm side. Used to control refinement along mask edges.

parameters.name: edge.mns
parameters.desc: List of material names for minimum-norm side edge processing.

mask.edge.ngr.description: Specifies the material names for mask edge processing on the no-growth-region side. Controls refinement along mask edges where no growth occurs.

parameters.name: edge.ngr
parameters.desc: List of material names for no-growth-region side edge processing.

mask.edge.refine.extent.description: Specifies the extent of refinement along mask edges. Controls how far from the edge boundary the mesh refinement extends.

parameters.name: edge.refine.extent
parameters.desc: Edge refinement extent distance (default unit: um).

mater.description: Returns a list of all materials in the current structure and adds new materials to a global list. Can compute the cropped bounding box of a material, set crystalline properties (lattice system, constants, orientations), and define material parameter inheritance.

parameters.name: add
parameters.desc: Adds a new material specified by name to the global material list.

parameters.name: Interface
parameters.desc: Returns a list of interface materials.

parameters.name: list.all
parameters.desc: Lists all defined materials.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the material.

parameters.name: new.like=
parameters.desc: Name of existing material to inherit default values from.

parameters.name: alt.matername=
parameters.desc: Alternative material name written to TDR files.

parameters.name: cubic
parameters.desc: Specifies cubic lattice system.

parameters.name: hexagonal
parameters.desc: Specifies hexagonal lattice system.

parameters.name: orthorhombic
parameters.desc: Specifies orthorhombic lattice system.

parameters.name: lattice.const=
parameters.desc: Lattice constant (default unit: um, default value: 5.431e-4).

parameters.name: lattice.const.b=
parameters.desc: Lattice constant b for non-cubic crystals (default unit: um).

parameters.name: lattice.const.c=
parameters.desc: Lattice constant c for non-cubic crystals (default unit: um).

parameters.name: polytype=
parameters.desc: Polytype: Sc, Bcc, Fcc, Zincblende (cubic); 2H, 3C, 4H, 6H (hexagonal).

parameters.name: vertical.orient=
parameters.desc: Wafer orientation as Miller indices (default: {0 0 1}).

parameters.name: horizontal.orient=
parameters.desc: Flat orientation as Miller indices.

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.desc: Returns the maximum extents of the material.

parameters.name: cropped.bbox
parameters.desc: Returns the cropped bounding box within a user-defined box.

mater.example:
mater add name= Germanium new.like= Silicon
mater name= GaN hexagonal polytype= 2H lattice.const= 3.189e-4 lattice.const.c= 5.185e-4 vertical.orient= {0 0 1} horizontal.orient= {1 1 0}

math.description: Sets numeric and matrix parameters including solver selection (PARDISO or ILS), Newton method options, time discretization schemes, coordinate systems, error control, and parallel processing configuration.

parameters.name: pardiso | ils
parameters.desc: Selects the linear solver: PARDISO (parallel direct) or ILS (iterative).

parameters.name: scale
parameters.desc: Applies row/column scaling to improve matrix conditioning.

parameters.name: fullNewton | modNewton
parameters.desc: Newton method type: full factorization at each step or reuse factorization across steps.

parameters.name: newtonDeriv
parameters.desc: Switches on Jacobian computation during modified Newton step. Default: false.

parameters.name: newtonRate1=
parameters.desc: Improvement factor for Newton steps 1 through newtonSteps1. Default: 4.0.

parameters.name: newtonRate2=
parameters.desc: Improvement factor for Newton steps after newtonSteps1. Default: 32.0.

parameters.name: newtonSteps1=
parameters.desc: Number of first modified Newton steps. Default: 12.

parameters.name: newtonTries1=
parameters.desc: Allowed breakdowns before switching to full Newton. Default: 2.

parameters.name: newtonStats
parameters.desc: Prints information on Newton iterations.

parameters.name: milne | difference
parameters.desc: Time step estimation method. Default: milne.

parameters.name: tr_bdf | euler
parameters.desc: Time discretization scheme: TR-BDF(2) or backward Euler. Default: tr_bdf.

parameters.name: extrapolate
parameters.desc: Uses extrapolated solutions as initial guess for BDF step.

parameters.name: auto | compatible
parameters.desc: CNR mode for Intel CPUs: auto or compatible (minimizes FMA differences). Default: compatible.

parameters.name: AMS.NegErrCntrl
parameters.desc: Stricter error control for adaptive meshing steps using negative updates.

parameters.name: FTS.NegErrCntrl
parameters.desc: Stricter error control for first solve time step.

parameters.name: LocTrnErrCntrl
parameters.desc: Stricter error control modifying handling of negative updates.

parameters.name: NegErrCntrl
parameters.desc: Stricter error control at each Newton iteration.

parameters.name: diffuse | Flow
parameters.desc: Applies specification to a specific equation type.

parameters.name: dimension=
parameters.desc: Applies specification to a specific dimension (1, 2, or 3).

parameters.name: numThreads=
parameters.desc: Number of parallel threads for Sentaurus Process.

parameters.name: numThreadsAssembly=
parameters.desc: Number of threads for matrix assembly.

parameters.name: numThreadsMC=
parameters.desc: Number of threads for Monte Carlo implantation.

parameters.name: numThreadsKMC=
parameters.desc: Number of threads for KMC diffusion.

parameters.name: numThreadsPardiso=
parameters.desc: Number of threads for PARDISO solver.

parameters.name: numThreadsILS=
parameters.desc: Number of threads for ILS solver.

parameters.name: numThreadsMGoals=
parameters.desc: Number of threads for MGOALS.

parameters.name: numThreadsSnMesh=
parameters.desc: Number of threads for Sentaurus Mesh.

parameters.name: coord.xyz | coord.yxz | coord.-zyx
parameters.desc: Coordinate system selection.

mechdata.description: Defines or queries mechanical data for materials used in stress simulations. Not documented as a standalone command in the User Guide appendix; typically material mechanical properties are configured through PDB parameters or the stressdata command.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material for which mechanical data is specified.

parameters.name: temperature=
parameters.desc: Temperature at which mechanical data applies (default unit: degree Celsius).

mgoals.description: Modifies the default parameters for geometric operations in the MGOALS module, including level-set accuracy, offset type, boundary repair, resolution, and algorithm selection for etching and deposition.

parameters.name: accuracy=
parameters.desc: Error tolerance in transferring interface definition from level-set grid to simulation grid. Default: 1.0e-5 um.

parameters.name: analytic.thickness=
parameters.desc: Thickness threshold below which analytic etching is used instead of level-set. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: aniso.etching.fragment.tol=
parameters.desc: Tolerance for removing fragments from 3D anisotropic etching. Default: 1.0e-6.

parameters.name: aniso.etching.protect.materials
parameters.desc: Protects areas shadowed by buried materials during 3D anisotropic etching.

parameters.name: dx= | dy= | dz=
parameters.desc: Explicitly set the level-set grid spacing in each direction.

parameters.name: fill.buried
parameters.desc: Deposits material inside buried gas bubbles.

parameters.name: fill.buried.interface=
parameters.desc: Material to fill gas bubbles at material interfaces.

parameters.name: fill.buried.match.surrounding
parameters.desc: Fills completely surrounded gas bubbles with the surrounding material.

parameters.name: force.analytic
parameters.desc: Forces the analytic algorithm even when boundary collision will occur.

parameters.name: force.full.levelset
parameters.desc: Sets the general level-set time-stepping algorithm as the default for both etching and deposition.

parameters.name: force.full.levelset.depo
parameters.desc: Sets full level-set for deposition only.

parameters.name: force.full.levelset.etch
parameters.desc: Sets full level-set for etching only.

parameters.name: fourier.local.diffusivity
parameters.desc: Controls the artificial diffusion parameter for Fourier rates.

parameters.name: full.resolution=
parameters.desc: Separate resolution setting for Fourier, crystallographic, and multimaterial etching. Default: 0.025 um.

parameters.name: keep.closed.voids
parameters.desc: Checks for enclosed voids at each crystallographic deposition time step.

parameters.name: levelset2019.2nd.order.scheme
parameters.desc: Activates the less diffusive second-order discretization scheme for the 3D level set engine.

parameters.name: max.number.levelset.cells=
parameters.desc: Maximum number of cells used by the level-set mesh. Default: 1.0e9.

parameters.name: min.gas.thickness=
parameters.desc: Minimum thickness of the gas layer at the top of the structure. Default: 0.1 um.

parameters.name: min.levelset.size=
parameters.desc: Minimum size for the level-set mesh. Default: 1.0e-4 um.

parameters.name: offset.adaptive.resolution=
parameters.desc: Size of the smallest cell during adaptive offsetting. Default: 0.5.

parameters.name: offset.type=
parameters.desc: Algorithm for isotropic deposition/etching: automatic, adaptive, analytic, automatic.adaptive, or fixed.

parameters.name: print.params
parameters.desc: Prints the current MGOALS parameters.

parameters.name: reinitfrequency=
parameters.desc: Level-set reinitialization frequency (0 = disabled). Default: 0.

parameters.name: reinititerations=
parameters.desc: Internal reinitialization algorithm iteration count. Default: 1.

parameters.name: remove.floating.regions
parameters.desc: Automatically removes regions not attached to the bottom. Default: true.

parameters.name: resolution=
parameters.desc: Minimum number of level-set cells across deposited/etched layer thickness. Default: 0.1.

parameters.name: trapezoidal.algorithm=
parameters.desc: Algorithm for trapezoidal etching (1 = old, 2 = new). Default: 2.

parameters.name: use.brep.2d
parameters.desc: Switches on the brep structure mode for 2D. Default: false.

parameters.name: use.levelset2019
parameters.desc: Switches to the Lax-Friedrichs level-set algorithm.

parameters.name: <version>
parameters.desc: Sets backward compatibility of parameters and algorithms to a specific version.

mgoals.native.description: Switches the mgoals meshing engine to use the native (built-in) mesher instead of the default MGOALS mesher. Used for compatibility with legacy meshing workflows.

optimize.description: Optimizes specified parameters to achieve required target values using an iterative loop. Requires a user-defined Tcl procedure that computes model results given parameters. Returns a Tcl list with optimized parameters, RMS error, and total loop count. Supports fitting problems with independent variable data from target files.

parameters.name: model.function=
parameters.desc: Name of a user-defined Tcl procedure that computes model results.

parameters.name: model.parameters=
parameters.desc: Names of parameters to be optimized.

parameters.name: target=
parameters.desc: Required target data values the optimization attempts to achieve.

parameters.name: target.file=
parameters.desc: Text file with two columns: independent variable and target data (e.g., SIMS data).

parameters.name: history=
parameters.desc: File where optimization history (parameters, targets, RMS errors) is stored.

parameters.name: max.iter=
parameters.desc: Maximum number of iterations (default: 500).

parameters.name: min.abs=
parameters.desc: Minimum absolute error threshold for each target (default: 1e-10).

parameters.name: min.rel=
parameters.desc: Minimum relative error threshold for each target (default: 1e-10).

parameters.name: param.init=
parameters.desc: Initial values of parameters (default: 0.5).

parameters.name: param.log=
parameters.desc: Names of parameters to vary logarithmically.

parameters.name: param.lower=
parameters.desc: Lower bound values for parameters (default: 0.0).

parameters.name: param.upper=
parameters.desc: Upper bound values for parameters (default: 1.0).

parameters.name: tolerance=
parameters.desc: RMS error convergence criterion (default: 0.1).

parameters.name: weight=
parameters.desc: Weighting factors for each target (default: 1.0 for all).

optimize.example: optimize model.function= optFunc model.parameters= {temp dose} param.init= {temp=1000 dose=1E14} param.lower= {temp=900 dose=1E12} param.upper= {temp=1200 dose=1E15} param.log= {dose} target= {0.06} max.iter= 500 tolerance= 0.1 history= test.dat

optimize.dislocation.description: Enables dislocation optimization during mechanical stress simulations. Adjusts the dislocation network to minimize system energy.

paste.description: Assembles 2D or 3D simulations by incorporating fragments from one or more TDR files. Appends structures to specified sides (left, right, front, or back) of the current simulation. Meshes are regenerated from combined boundary geometry.

parameters.name: tdr=
parameters.desc: List of TDR file names (prefixes) to import and paste.

parameters.name: direction=
parameters.desc: List of sides where to paste: left, right, front, or back. Must match tdr count.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing for the pasted structure.

paste.example:
paste tdr = "propertyx" direction = "right"
paste tdr = {propertyx1 propertyx2} direction = {right right}

pdbDelayDouble.description: Retrieves an expression for a double parameter that will be evaluated at each time step during diffusion. Typically used in Alagator to retrieve Arrhenius parameter expressions where temperature may change during a diffusion step.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name (e.g., Silicon, Si).

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name (e.g., Boron, B, Interstitial).

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical parameter path in the PDB.

pdbDopantLike.description: Creates a new dopant in a material by inheriting properties from existing dopant infrastructure. The new dopant can then use diffusion switches such as DiffModel and ActiveModel.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name (e.g., Silicon).

parameters.name: <dopant>
parameters.desc: Name of the new dopant to create.

pdbExprDouble.description: Retrieves an expression for a double parameter without evaluating it. Used in Alagator to retrieve parameter expressions that depend on solution names or data fields, where evaluation must be delayed until solution time.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical parameter path in the PDB.

pdbGet.description: Retrieves a parameter value from the parameter database with automatic type checking. Exits with a list of available parameters if the parameter is not found. Must replace all other pdbGet* commands in command files because type and syntax are checked automatically.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: First level of the hierarchical path (e.g., Mechanics, Silicon, Diffuse).

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Remaining hierarchical path to the parameter.

pdbGetArray.description: Retrieves an array parameter from the parameter database without syntax checking. Returns 0 if the parameter is not found.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: First level of the hierarchical path.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the array parameter.

pdbGetBoolean.description: Retrieves a Boolean parameter from the parameter database without syntax checking. Returns 0 if the parameter is not found. Preferred for Alagator scripting.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: First level of the hierarchical path.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the Boolean parameter.

pdbGetDouble.description: Retrieves a double-precision floating-point parameter from the parameter database without syntax checking. Returns 0 if the parameter is not found. Preferred for Alagator scripting.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name (e.g., Silicon, Si).

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name (e.g., Boron, B).

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Parameter name in the PDB hierarchy.

pdbGetDoubleArray.description: Retrieves a double-precision array parameter from the parameter database without syntax checking. Returns 0 if the parameter is not found.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the double array parameter.

pdbGetElement.description: Retrieves the value of one element in a PDB array by index.

parameters.name: <array>
parameters.desc: Name of the array parameter.

parameters.name: <index>
parameters.desc: Array index of the element to retrieve.

pdbGetFunction.description: Retrieves a function expression parameter from the parameter database without syntax checking. Returns 0 if the parameter is not found.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the function parameter.

pdbGetString.description: Retrieves a string parameter from the parameter database without syntax checking. Returns 0 if the parameter is not found. Preferred for Alagator scripting.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the string parameter.

pdbGetSwitch.description: Retrieves the integer value of a switch parameter from the parameter database. Returns the zero-based index of the selected choice. Returns 0 if the parameter is not found.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the switch parameter.

pdbGetSwitchString.description: Retrieves the string value of a switch parameter from the parameter database. For example, if a switch has choices a, b, or c and a is chosen, returns the string a. Returns nothing if the parameter is not found.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the switch parameter.

pdbIsAvailable.description: Checks whether a given parameter exists in the parameter database. Returns 1 if the parameter exists, 0 otherwise.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: First level of the hierarchical path.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Remaining hierarchical path to check.

pdbLike.description: Creates a new parameter node in the PDB that inherits all sub-parameters from an existing node. Typically used to create new solution variables that inherit parameters from existing ones, such as a new dopant based on an existing species.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name (e.g., Silicon).

parameters.name: <new_name>
parameters.desc: Name of the new PDB parameter to create.

parameters.name: <existing_name>
parameters.desc: Name of the existing PDB parameter to inherit from.

pdbSet.description: Sets a parameter value in the parameter database with automatic type checking. Exits with a list of available parameters if the parameter is not found. Must replace all other pdbSet* commands in command files because the type and syntax are checked automatically.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: First level of the hierarchical path.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the parameter.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Value to set. The type must match the parameter type (double, string, Boolean, switch, or array).

pdbSetArray.description: Defines or modifies a string data array in the parameter database. Creates the array if it does not already exist.

parameters.name: <array_name>
parameters.desc: Name of the array to create or modify.

parameters.name: <value_list>
parameters.desc: Tcl list of index-value pairs (e.g., {0 abc 1 def 2 ghi}).

pdbSetBoolean.description: Sets a Boolean parameter in the parameter database. Creates the parameter if it does not already exist. Does not exit on missing parameters. Preferred for Alagator scripting.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the Boolean parameter.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Boolean value (1 or 0).

pdbSetDouble.description: Sets a double-precision floating-point parameter in the parameter database. Creates the parameter if it does not already exist. Does not exit on missing parameters. Preferred for Alagator scripting. The PDB uses the CGS system of units, except for activation energies [eV].

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name (e.g., Silicon, Si).

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name (e.g., Boron, B).

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Parameter name in the PDB hierarchy.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Double-precision value to set. Can be an expression or Arrhenius form.

pdbSetDoubleArray.description: Sets a double-precision array parameter in the parameter database. Creates the array if it does not already exist. Supports charge-state-dependent parameters where the array index refers to the charge state, and double-sum arrays with comma-separated index fields.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the double array parameter.

parameters.name: <value_list>
parameters.desc: Tcl list of index-value pairs (e.g., {0 0.1 1 0.2 3 0.3}).

pdbSetElement.description: Modifies the value of one element in an existing PDB array by index.

parameters.name: <array>
parameters.desc: Name of the array parameter.

parameters.name: <index>
parameters.desc: Array index of the element to modify.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: New value for the element.

pdbSetFunction.description: Sets a function expression parameter in the parameter database. Creates the parameter if it does not already exist.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the function parameter.

parameters.name: <expression>
parameters.desc: Function expression to store (e.g., Arrhenius form, Tcl expression).

pdbSetString.description: Sets a string parameter in the parameter database. Creates the parameter if it does not already exist. Commonly used to set equation strings for user-defined models in Alagator.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name (e.g., Silicon).

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or solution variable name (e.g., M, Boron).

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Parameter name (e.g., Equation, EquationProc).

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: String value to set.

pdbSetSwitch.description: Sets the value of an existing switch parameter or creates a new switch if one does not exist. Switch parameters are enumerated choices (e.g., DiffModel: Pair, Fermi, etc.).

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or category name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the switch parameter.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Switch choice name.

pdbUnSetBoolean.description: Temporarily removes a Boolean parameter from the parameter database during the simulation. The parameter is removed until explicitly set again.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the Boolean parameter.

pdbUnSetDouble.description: Temporarily removes a double-precision parameter from the parameter database during the simulation.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the double parameter.

pdbUnSetDoubleArray.description: Temporarily removes a double-precision array parameter from the parameter database during the simulation.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the double array parameter.

pdbUnSetString.description: Temporarily removes a string parameter from the parameter database during the simulation.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name.

parameters.name: <species>
parameters.desc: Species or dopant name.

parameters.name: <parameter>
parameters.desc: Full hierarchical path to the string parameter.

pdbdiff.description: Compares the current structure with one from a TDR file and reports differences between stored parameters and their values.

parameters.name: <c>
parameters.desc: Full path or prefix of the first TDR file (filename without _fps.tdr).

parameters.name: <c>
parameters.desc: Full path or prefix of the second TDR file (filename without _fps.tdr).

photo.description: Creates a photoresist layer of the specified thickness outside the defined mask. The photoresist is deposited in the openings of the mask. Use the negative argument in the mask command to deposit inside the mask instead.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing for photoresist deposition.

parameters.name: angle=
parameters.desc: Angle of the sidewalls in degrees (default unit: degree).

parameters.name: mask=
parameters.desc: Name of the mask used to define the photoresist pattern.

parameters.name: repair
parameters.desc: Controls removal of small regions in 3D MGOALS mode.

parameters.name: sde=
parameters.desc: SDE algorithm specification for 3D simulations.

parameters.name: thickness=
parameters.desc: Photoresist thickness (default: 2.0 um).

parameters.name: trapezoidal.algorithm=
parameters.desc: Algorithm for tapered shape: default, offset, or straight.skeleton.

plot.1d.description: Plots 1D cross sections vertically or horizontally through the device. Supports axis configuration, material boundary markers, and line customization for the plot window.

parameters.name: boundary
parameters.desc: Draws vertical lines at material boundaries on the plot. Default: false.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears the graphics screen before drawing. Default: true.

parameters.name: close
parameters.desc: Closes the plot window.

parameters.name: color=
parameters.desc: Line color for the plot. Any X11 color name.

parameters.name: fix.ratio
parameters.desc: Fixes the x- to y-axis ratio. Default: false.

parameters.name: label=
parameters.desc: Name of the line in the plot legend. Default: current dataset name.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: List of numeric values for x- and y-axis maximum ends. First value is x, second is y.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: List of numeric values for x- and y-axis minimum ends. First value is x, second is y.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of a data field to plot without using the select command. Default: Z_Plot_Var.

parameters.name: rescale
parameters.desc: Rescales the plot to fit the entire simulation domain.

parameters.name: symb=
parameters.desc: Symbol type drawn at each data point on the cross-sectional line.

parameters.name: title=
parameters.desc: Title of the plot window.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: Constant x-coordinate for vertical slice (2D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Constant y-coordinate for horizontal slice (2D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Constant z-coordinate for cutline (3D). Default unit: um.

plot.2d.description: Plots the 2D structure showing material boundaries, grid, vector fields, and node/edge/face indices. Typically used to visualize the device geometry and mesh.

parameters.name: boundary
parameters.desc: Draws device outline and material interfaces. Default: false.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears the graphics screen before drawing. Default: true.

parameters.name: close
parameters.desc: Closes the plot window.

parameters.name: col.bound=
parameters.desc: Color of the boundary lines. Any valid X11 color.

parameters.name: col.grid=
parameters.desc: Color of the grid lines. Any valid X11 color.

parameters.name: edges
parameters.desc: Prints edge indices on the plot. Default: false.

parameters.name: faces
parameters.desc: Prints face indices on the plot. Default: false.

parameters.name: fill
parameters.desc: Expands the device to fill the screen. Default: false (proper aspect ratio).

parameters.name: fix.ratio
parameters.desc: Fixes the x to y ratio. Use !fix.ratio to switch off. Default: true.

parameters.name: gas
parameters.desc: Plots the grid in the gas region. Default: false.

parameters.name: grid
parameters.desc: Draws the numeric grid on which the problem was solved. Default: false.

parameters.name: kmc
parameters.desc: Plots particles in atomistic KMC simulations as dots.

parameters.name: label.bound
parameters.desc: Prints the material name in the lower-left corner of each material region.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: List of numeric values for x- and y-axis maximum ends. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: List of numeric values for x- and y-axis minimum ends. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: nodes
parameters.desc: Prints node indices on the plot. Default: false.

parameters.name: rescale
parameters.desc: Rescales the plot to fit the whole simulation domain.

parameters.name: title=
parameters.desc: Title of the plot window.

parameters.name: vector=
parameters.desc: Name of a vector field to display as arrows. Does not work in 3D.

parameters.name: vlength=
parameters.desc: Scales the maximum vector length. Default: 0.1 um.

parameters.name: vmax=
parameters.desc: Uses this as the maximum velocity instead of searching. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: x=|y=|z=
parameters.desc: Cutline through the structure for 3D simulations. One must be specified. Default unit: um.

plot.xy.description: Configures a 2D plot surface for use with the point.xy command. Prepares axis scaling, labels, and controls log axes for custom xy plotting.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears the existing plot surface. Default: true.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: List of numeric values for x- and y-axis maximum ends. First is x-value, second is y-value. Default: maximum extent of current structure.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: List of numeric values for x- and y-axis minimum ends. First is x-value, second is y-value. Default: minimum extent of current structure.

parameters.name: x.axis=
parameters.desc: Label for the x-axis.

parameters.name: y.axis=
parameters.desc: Label for the y-axis.

parameters.name: x.log
parameters.desc: Uses logarithmic scale for the x-axis.

parameters.name: y.log
parameters.desc: Uses logarithmic scale for the y-axis.

plot.xy.example: plot.xy min= {1 0.0} max= {3600 0.75} x.axis= Time y.axis= Thickness

point.description: Defines a named point in 3D space or a 2D point in the yz plane. Used to construct polygons for masks or other geometric definitions.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the point.

parameters.name: coord=
parameters.desc: Coordinates of the point. Two values for 2D (y,z), three for 3D (y,z,x). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears all defined points or the named point only.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a list of all currently defined points.

point.coord.description: Specifies the coordinates of a named point. Points are used as building blocks for polygon and mask definitions.

parameters.name: coord
parameters.desc: Coordinate values (2D: x y, 3D: x y z).

point.implant.description: Defines a point used for implantation reference. Used to specify spatial reference points for implant operations.

point.response.description: Defines a point used for response surface extraction. Used to specify evaluation points for response analysis.

point.xy.description: Adds data points to a named line on a 1D or 2D plot. Used to plot calculated values, simulation data, or direct outputs. Supports scatter plots via the move option.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: X-value to add to the plot.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Y-value to add to the plot.

parameters.name: color=
parameters.desc: Line color (X11 color name).

parameters.name: move
parameters.desc: Places the pen at this point without drawing from the last point (scatter mode).

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the line to add points to. Creates the line if it does not exist.

parameters.name: symb=
parameters.desc: Symbol character for marking points. First character is used. Default: x.

polygon.signature: polygon clear | list | (name=<c> [rectangle | points= {<pt1> <pt2> ...} | segments= {<y1> <z1> <y2> <z2> ...} | tdr=<c> [materials= {<mat1> ...}] | min= {<y> <z>} max= {<y> <z>}] [xy])
polygon.description: Creates a polygon for use as a mask or during insertion. Polygons can be defined from named points, segments, rectangles, or loaded from TDR files. The polygon is closed by connecting the first and last points implicitly.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the polygon.

parameters.name: points=
parameters.desc: List of named points (minimum 3) defining the polygon.

parameters.name: segments=
parameters.desc: List of y/z coordinate pairs defining line segments in the yz plane. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: rectangle
parameters.desc: Creates a rectangular polygon when used with points (2 pts) or min/max.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: Minimum corner {y z} for a rectangular polygon. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: Maximum corner {y z} for a rectangular polygon. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: tdr=
parameters.desc: TDR file to read the polygon from (requires xy).

parameters.name: materials=
parameters.desc: Materials to include when reading from TDR.

parameters.name: regions=
parameters.desc: Regions to include when reading from TDR.

parameters.name: xy
parameters.desc: Defines polygon in the xy plane instead of yz (required for tdr and insertion).

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears all or the named polygon.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a list of all defined polygons.

parameters.name: external.sde
parameters.desc: Creates polygon from an external SDE structure.

polygon.bounding.boxes.description: Returns the bounding boxes of all defined polygons. Useful for querying the spatial extent of polygonal regions.

polygon.inside.points.description: Tests which polygons contain given points. Returns the polygon names that enclose the specified coordinates.

polygon.name.description: Specifies the name of a polygon for identification. Named polygons can be referenced by mask and other geometry commands.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Name identifier for the polygon.

polygon.name.example: polygon name= GatePoly points= {p1 p2 p3 p4}

polygon.names.description: Returns a list of all defined polygon names. Used to query existing polygon definitions in the simulation.

polygon.tessellations.description: Returns the tessellation (triangulation) of defined polygons. Used for mesh generation from polygonal boundaries.

polyhedron.signature: polyhedron clear | list | (name=<c> [brick= {<minx> <miny> <minz> <maxx> <maxy> <maxz>} | original.polyhedron=<c> | (polygons= <list> [min=<n>][<um>] [max=<n>][<um>]) | (tdr=<c> [materials=<list>] | [regions=<list>])] [bbox | bbox.cm | bbox.um] [decimation] [decimation.accuracy=<n>][<um>] [external.sde] [offset= {<x> <y> <z>}] [rotate.angles= {<psi> <theta> <phi>}] [rotate.center= {<x> <y> <z>}] [scale=<n>] [scale.center= {<x> <y> <z>}] [stretch] [direction= x|-x|y|-y|z|-z] [length=<n>] [location=<n>] [check.topology])
polyhedron.description: Creates and stores 3D polyhedra for later insertion with the insert command or use with the profile command. Supports creation from bricks, extruded polygons, polygonal faces, TDR files, or other existing polyhedra. Transformations such as offset, rotate, scale, and stretch can be applied.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the polyhedron to create.

parameters.name: brick=
parameters.desc: Two corners {minx miny minz maxx maxy maxz} defining a rectangular prism. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: polygons=
parameters.desc: List of polygon names to form a polyhedron, or one polygon to extrude.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: Minimum x-coordinate for extrusion. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate for extrusion. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: tdr=
parameters.desc: TDR boundary file to read polyhedra from.

parameters.name: materials=
parameters.desc: Materials to include from TDR file.

parameters.name: regions=
parameters.desc: Regions to include from TDR file.

parameters.name: original.polyhedron=
parameters.desc: Creates new polyhedron from an existing one before applying transformations.

parameters.name: offset=
parameters.desc: Translation offset {x y z} before saving.

parameters.name: rotate.angles=
parameters.desc: Tait-Bryan rotation angles {psi theta phi} (x, y', z'' axes).

parameters.name: rotate.center=
parameters.desc: Rotation center {x y z}. Default: geometric center.

parameters.name: scale=
parameters.desc: Proportional scaling factor.

parameters.name: stretch
parameters.desc: Stretches the polyhedron along a direction.

parameters.name: direction=
parameters.desc: Stretch direction: x, -x, y, -y, z, or -z. Default: x.

parameters.name: length=
parameters.desc: Stretch length. Default: 0.

parameters.name: location=
parameters.desc: Stretch location. Default: 0.

parameters.name: decimation
parameters.desc: Performs decimation after loading and transformations. Default: false.

parameters.name: decimation.accuracy=
parameters.desc: Decimation accuracy. Default: 1e-4 um.

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.desc: Returns maximum extents of the polyhedron in two points.

parameters.name: check.topology
parameters.desc: Performs topological checks to validate polyhedron is closed.

parameters.name: external.sde
parameters.desc: Creates polyhedron from external SDE structure.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Removes all defined polyhedra from memory.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Lists currently defined polyhedra.

polyhedron.material.description: Specifies the material type for a polyhedron region. Used in 3D simulations to assign material properties to polyhedral volumes.

parameters.name: material
parameters.desc: Material name for the polyhedron (e.g., Silicon, Oxide).

print.1d.description: Prints values along a 1D cross section. Returns a Tcl list for all values, allowing subsequent processing such as integration. Particularly useful for creating input for xy plots.

parameters.name: gas
parameters.desc: Includes gas mesh data in the extracted output. Default: false.

parameters.name: interfaces
parameters.desc: Prints interface data for the field specified by name, organized by interface.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Name of the material for which data fields are printed.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of a data field to print without using the select command. Default: current selected field.

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Name of the region for which data fields are printed.

parameters.name: syntax.check.value=
parameters.desc: Value to return during syntax-checking mode to avoid false errors.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: Constant x-coordinate for vertical slice (2D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Constant y-coordinate for horizontal slice (2D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Constant z-coordinate for cutline (3D). Default unit: um.

print.1d.example:
print.1d x= 1.0
print.1d name= Boron x=0.0 Silicon

print.commands.description: Controls whether Sentaurus Process commands are echoed to the screen and log file. Equivalent to the PrintCommands option of the LogFile command. Use !PrintCommands via LogFile to suppress command echo.

print.data.description: Writes data in x-, y-, z-format to a file. Used primarily to export a data field for use with 3D plotting tools.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of a data field to print. Default: Z_Plot_Var.

parameters.name: outfile=
parameters.desc: Name of the output file. Previous content is destroyed.

parameters.name: xlo=
parameters.desc: Minimum x-coordinate of the 3D bounding box. Default: 0 um.

parameters.name: xhi=
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate of the 3D bounding box. Default: 0 um.

parameters.name: ylo=
parameters.desc: Minimum y-coordinate of the 3D bounding box. Default: 0 um.

parameters.name: yhi=
parameters.desc: Maximum y-coordinate of the 3D bounding box. Default: 0 um.

parameters.name: zlo=
parameters.desc: Minimum z-coordinate of the 3D bounding box. Default: 0 um.

parameters.name: zhi=
parameters.desc: Maximum z-coordinate of the 3D bounding box. Default: 0 um.

print_interpolated_params.description: Prints the parameters that have been read and to which mole fraction-dependent interpolation has been applied. Returns results as a Tcl list. Must be called after interpolation has occurred (e.g., after a diffuse command).

parameters.name: Diffuse
parameters.desc: Prints mole fraction-dependent diffusion parameters, including any unmatched by other options.

parameters.name: KMC
parameters.desc: Prints mole fraction-dependent KMC parameters.

parameters.name: Mechanics
parameters.desc: Prints mole fraction-dependent mechanics parameters.

profile.description: Reads a data file and constructs a data field. Supports reading doping profiles from SIMS measurements or TDR files, placing uniform dopant concentrations inside polyhedra, and generating 2D/3D distributions by combining 1D profiles with mask information via Gaussian convolution.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the data field to create or modify.

parameters.name: infile=
parameters.desc: Name of the ASCII data file or TDR file to read.

parameters.name: concentration=
parameters.desc: Concentration value(s) at xcoord or inside polyhedron.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Performs profile placement with adaptive remeshing.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material to which the field profile is applied.

parameters.name: logarithmic
parameters.desc: Logarithmic interpolation (default).

parameters.name: linear
parameters.desc: Linear interpolation.

parameters.name: arsinh
parameters.desc: Inverse hyperbolic sine interpolation.

parameters.name: mask=
parameters.desc: Mask name for 2D Gaussian blurring.

parameters.name: gaussian.blur
parameters.desc: Generates 2D/3D distribution by combining 1D profile with mask information.

parameters.name: polyhedron=
parameters.desc: Name of a defined polyhedron for placing uniform dopant.

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Region name to limit the profile application.

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Replaces existing dataset with new concentration.

parameters.name: sum
parameters.desc: Adds new concentration to existing dataset (default).

parameters.name: x.sigma=
parameters.desc: Standard deviation of erfc falloff in x-direction (default unit: um).

parameters.name: xcoord=
parameters.desc: X-coordinates for profile definition (default unit: um).

parameters.name: xscale=
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for the depth dimension (default: 1.0).

parameters.name: y.sigma=
parameters.desc: Standard deviation in y-direction (defaults to x.sigma).

parameters.name: z.sigma=
parameters.desc: Standard deviation in z-direction (defaults to y.sigma).

profile.example:
profile name= Boron infile= utmar.bor xscale= 1.0e-4
profile name= Data infile= SIMS

reaction.description: Defines reacting materials and the new material that forms as the product of the reaction. Used for continuum moving-boundary simulations (oxidation, silicidation, epitaxy) and LKMC chemical vapor epitaxy reactions (adsorption, surface reaction, desorption, etching).

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Lists the names of already defined reactions.

parameters.name: mat.l=
parameters.desc: Material on the left side of the interface (alphabetically first).

parameters.name: mat.r=
parameters.desc: Material on the right side of the interface.

parameters.name: mat.new=
parameters.desc: Product material of the reaction.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the reaction.

parameters.name: ambient.name=
parameters.desc: Specifies an ambient-type reaction and which ambient must be present.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears the diffusing species list or deletes all reactions.

parameters.name: delete
parameters.desc: Deletes the named reaction.

parameters.name: diffusing.species=
parameters.desc: List of reactants for material growth reactions.

parameters.name: multiphase
parameters.desc: Switches on multiphase reaction such as silicidation of nickel.

parameters.name: KMC.species=
parameters.desc: KMC species for atomistic subsurface LKMC reactions.

parameters.name: energy=
parameters.desc: Additional energy barrier for LKMC reactions (Coordinations.Reactions model).

parameters.name: prefactor=
parameters.desc: Prefactor for LKMC reaction rates.

parameters.name: lkmc
parameters.desc: Specifies LKMC reaction parameters block.

reaction.example: reaction mat.l= Silicon mat.r= Oxide mat.new= Oxide name= SiOx

refinebox.description: Defines mesh refinement boxes to control local mesh resolution. Supports spatial refinement, interface refinement, adaptive meshing criteria, and mask-driven refinement with edge and corner control.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement box.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Deletes all previously defined refinement boxes, or the named one if name is specified.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Lists all defined refinement boxes.

parameters.name: print
parameters.desc: Prints information for all or named refinement boxes.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Specifies an adaptive refinement box used during remeshing operations.

parameters.name: 3d | !3d
parameters.desc: Limits refinement box to 3D only or to 1D/2D only.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: Tcl list of minimum coordinates (x, y, z) of the box. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: Tcl list of maximum coordinates (x, y, z) of the box. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: xrefine=
parameters.desc: Three numbers for element sizes in x-direction (top, middle, bottom). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: yrefine=
parameters.desc: Three numbers for element sizes in y-direction (left, middle, right). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: zrefine=
parameters.desc: Three numbers for element sizes in z-direction (front, middle, back). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Limits the refinement box to a particular material.

parameters.name: materials=
parameters.desc: Limits the refinement box to a list of materials.

parameters.name: regions=
parameters.desc: Limits the refinement box to a list of regions.

parameters.name: mask=
parameters.desc: Uses an existing mask name as a spatial constraint for the refinement.

parameters.name: extrusion.min=
parameters.desc: Minimum x-coordinate when using mask-driven refinement. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: extrusion.max=
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate when using mask-driven refinement. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: extend=
parameters.desc: Extension distance for mask-driven refinement (positive or negative). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: mask.edge.mns=
parameters.desc: Minimum mesh size near mask edge. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: mask.edge.refine.extent=
parameters.desc: Distance from mask edge for edge-based refinement. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: mask.edge.ngr=
parameters.desc: Growth rate of refinement away from mask edge. Default: 1.0.

parameters.name: mask.corner.mns=
parameters.desc: Minimum mesh size near mask corner. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: mask.corner.refine.extent=
parameters.desc: Distance from mask corner for corner-based refinement. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: mask.corner.ngr=
parameters.desc: Growth rate of refinement away from mask corner. Default: 1.0.

parameters.name: double.side | !double.side
parameters.desc: Controls symmetric interpretation of material/region pairs. Default: double.side.

parameters.name: kmc
parameters.desc: Refines only the internal KMC boxes.

parameters.name: interface.materials=
parameters.desc: Refines all interfaces containing any of the listed materials.

parameters.name: interface.mat.pairs=
parameters.desc: Pairs of materials where interface meshing is switched on.

parameters.name: interface.region.pairs=
parameters.desc: Pairs of regions where interface meshing is switched on.

parameters.name: interface.regions=
parameters.desc: Switches on offsetting on a region-wise basis.

parameters.name: offsetting
parameters.desc: Uses Sentaurus Mesh offsetting algorithm for interface refinement.

parameters.name: offsetting.maxlevel=
parameters.desc: Number of offsetting layers at the interface.

parameters.name: min.normal.size=
parameters.desc: Minimum edge spacing at interfaces for this box. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: max.lateral.size=
parameters.desc: Maximum lateral spacing at the interface. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: normal.growth.ratio=
parameters.desc: Edge-to-edge growth ratio moving away from an interface.

parameters.name: normal.growth.depth=
parameters.desc: Maximum depth of refinement away from an interface. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: DelPSC
parameters.desc: Applies the DelPSC algorithm to rediscretize curved surfaces on the boundary.

parameters.name: def.rel.error=
parameters.desc: Default relative error for adaptive refinement.

parameters.name: def.abs.error=
parameters.desc: Default absolute error for adaptive refinement.

parameters.name: def.max.gradient=
parameters.desc: Default maximum gradient criteria for adaptive refinement.

parameters.name: def.max.dose.error=
parameters.desc: Default maximum local dose error criteria.

parameters.name: target.length=
parameters.desc: Target length for interval refinement. Default unit: um.

refinebox.example: refinebox Silicon min= {0.0 0.05} max= {0.1 0.12} xrefine= {0.01 0.01 0.01} yrefine= {0.1 0.005 0.1}

region.signature: region <material> xlo=<c> [ylo=<c>] [zlo=<c>] xhi=<c> [yhi=<c>] [zhi=<c>] [alt.matername=<c>] [bbox | bbox.cm | bbox.um] [change.material] [cropped.bbox] [exact.name] [field=<c> (resistivity=<n>[<ohm-cm>] | concentration=<n>)] [left | right | back | front | top | bottom] [list | list.bulk | list.gas | list.interface] [material] [max= {<n> <n> <n>}] [min= {<n> <n> <n>}] [name=<c>] [new.name=<c>] [point= {<n> <n> <n>}] [substrate] [use.icwb.domain] [volume] [zero.data]
region.description: Creates initial regions in concert with line and init commands, changes the material of existing regions, or queries region properties. Region bounds are specified using tags from preceding line commands.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material of the region.

parameters.name: xlo=
parameters.desc: Tag for the lower x-bound (from line command).

parameters.name: xhi=
parameters.desc: Tag for the upper x-bound (from line command).

parameters.name: ylo=
parameters.desc: Tag for the lower y-bound.

parameters.name: yhi=
parameters.desc: Tag for the upper y-bound.

parameters.name: zlo=
parameters.desc: Tag for the lower z-bound.

parameters.name: zhi=
parameters.desc: Tag for the upper z-bound.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the region (no underscore or period allowed).

parameters.name: change.material
parameters.desc: Changes the material of an existing region.

parameters.name: substrate
parameters.desc: Tags the region as the substrate for analysis.

parameters.name: field=
parameters.desc: Name of a field to initialize within the region.

parameters.name: concentration=
parameters.desc: Field concentration value. Default unit: cm-3.

parameters.name: resistivity=
parameters.desc: Sets field value by resistivity. Unit: ohm-cm.

parameters.name: zero.data
parameters.desc: Zeroes all data when changing material. Default: true.

parameters.name: alt.matername=
parameters.desc: Alternative material name for TDR file output.

parameters.name: volume
parameters.desc: Returns the volume of the named region.

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.desc: Returns maximum extents of the region.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a list of all regions.

parameters.name: list.bulk
parameters.desc: Returns a list of bulk (nongas) regions.

parameters.name: list.gas
parameters.desc: Returns a list of gas regions.

parameters.name: new.name=
parameters.desc: New name for renaming a region.

parameters.name: point=
parameters.desc: Point within a region for renaming.

parameters.name: exact.name
parameters.desc: Only changes the exact named region, not ancestors. Default: false.

parameters.name: use.icwb.domain
parameters.desc: Uses ICWB domain to define y/z bounds automatically.

region.example:
region silicon ylo= left yhi= right xlo= surf xhi= back
region name= bulk MySilicon change.material !zero.data

sde.description: Dispatches commands to Sentaurus Structure Editor for 3D geometry modeling. When enabled with 'on', all 3D geometry operations (deposit, etch, photo, strip, transform) are performed using SDE. Scheme commands can be sent directly inside braces.

parameters.name: {<SDE commands>}
parameters.desc: Scheme commands enclosed in braces to send to Sentaurus Structure Editor.

parameters.name: on
parameters.desc: Switches on SDE for all 3D geometry modeling.

parameters.name: off
parameters.desc: Switches off SDE mode (operations use MGOALS).

parameters.name: logfile=
parameters.desc: Name of the file to log all Scheme commands (recommended .scm extension).

parameters.name: external
parameters.desc: Puts SDE in external mode for creating polyhedra in MGOALS.

parameters.name: polyhedron=
parameters.desc: Polyhedron for initializing the external SDE interface.

parameters.name: polyhedron.material=
parameters.desc: Material of the external polyhedron. Default: Silicon.

parameters.name: remesh
parameters.desc: Forces a remesh at the end of the sde command.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing if remesh is given.

parameters.name: SdeCheck
parameters.desc: Performs geometry check on every boundary file created by SDE.

sel.description: Abbreviation for the select command with Tcl expression support. Unlike select, it accepts any general Tcl expression. Data fields are made into Tcl variables and accessed with standard Tcl variable methods. A companion to the select command.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a list of currently defined data fields.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the new data field (default: Z_Plot_Var).

parameters.name: store
parameters.desc: Writes the data field to permanent storage (default: false).

parameters.name: vec
parameters.desc: Lists vector data fields.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Tcl expression used to build a new data field.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material to which the command applies.

select.description: Selects the variable for display in all postprocessing commands. Creates new data fields and manipulates existing fields using Alagator language expressions. Can list existing fields, delete fields, and compute min/max values. Abbreviated as sel.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Alagator expression to build a new data field from existing fields.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the new or target data field (default: Z_Plot_Var).

parameters.name: delete
parameters.desc: Deletes the data field specified by name.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a list of currently defined real data fields.

parameters.name: list.all
parameters.desc: Returns a list of all data fields (real and vector).

parameters.name: store
parameters.desc: Sets the field to be written into permanent storage on struct output.

parameters.name: element
parameters.desc: Computes the field on elements.

parameters.name: edge.vector
parameters.desc: Computes weighted field with respect to edge orientation.

parameters.name: continuous
parameters.desc: Computes a continuous flux for a field gradient.

parameters.name: min
parameters.desc: Returns the minimum of the named field.

parameters.name: max
parameters.desc: Returns the maximum of the named field.

parameters.name: report.location
parameters.desc: Reports coordinates of the min/max value.

parameters.name: present
parameters.desc: Returns 1 if the named data field exists.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.desc: Returns 1 if the field is written to permanent storage.

parameters.name: value=
parameters.desc: Alternative to z for specifying the expression.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material to which the command applies.

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Region to which the command applies.

select.example:
select z = log10(Boron)
sel z= (MyData - 5.0e14) name= MyField store
select name= MyField delete

setMobilityModel.description: Sets the mobility model and related parameters for silicon and polysilicon for antimony, arsenic, boron, indium, and phosphorus dopants. Used to compute resistivity from doping concentration. Set values are printed in the log file.

parameters.name: <model_name>
parameters.desc: Mobility model name: Antoniadis (or Model1), Masetti (or Model2), Thurber (or Model3).

simDelayDouble.description: Retrieves the Tcl expression used to evaluate a double-precision simulator state variable without evaluating it. Similar to simGetDouble, but the evaluation of the returned expression is delayed.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: Category level (e.g., Diffuse).

parameters.name: <variable>
parameters.desc: Simulator state variable name (e.g., tempC, EpiThick, AnnealStepTime).

simGetBoolean.description: Reads a global simulator state Boolean variable. Available variables: AmbientReactions, IsEpi, IsGrowing, laser, MaterialReactions.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: Category level (e.g., Diffuse).

parameters.name: <variable>
parameters.desc: Boolean state variable name.

simGetDouble.description: Reads a double-precision global simulator state variable and returns its value. Available variables: EpiThick, PH2O, pO2, pressure, ramprate, temp, tempC, tempK, time, Vt, Vti. Returns zero if the variable is not defined.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: Category level (e.g., Diffuse, Heat).

parameters.name: <variable>
parameters.desc: Simulator state variable name.

simSetBoolean.description: Sets a global simulator state Boolean variable. Available variables: AmbientReactions, IsEpi, IsGrowing, laser, MaterialReactions. Modifying global state variables might cause errors in the simulation.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: Category level (e.g., Diffuse).

parameters.name: <variable>
parameters.desc: Boolean state variable name.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Boolean value (1 or 0).

simSetDouble.description: Sets a double-precision global simulator state variable. Available variables: PH2O, pO2, temp, tempC, tempK, Vti. Modifying global state variables might cause errors in the simulation.

parameters.name: <category>
parameters.desc: Category level (e.g., Diffuse).

parameters.name: <variable>
parameters.desc: Double state variable name.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Double-precision value to set.

slice.description: Extracts a 1D data slice through a 2D or 3D simulation object. Returns coordinate-value pairs where the coordinate is the distance along the cutline and the value is the local value of the selected quantity. Can also extract constant contour coordinates.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material to extract data from.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the data field. Default: Z_Plot_Var.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: Constant x-coordinate for the cutline (2D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Constant y-coordinate for the cutline (2D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Constant z-coordinate for the cutline (3D). Default unit: um.

parameters.name: value=
parameters.desc: Value for extracting constant contour coordinates.

parameters.name: p1=
parameters.desc: Start point {x y z} for arbitrary cutline.

parameters.name: p2=
parameters.desc: End point {x y z} for arbitrary cutline.

parameters.name: include.interfaces
parameters.desc: Includes interface values in returned data.

parameters.name: only.interfaces
parameters.desc: Returns interface values exclusively.

parameters.name: side=
parameters.desc: Bulk material side for interface value extraction.

parameters.name: mat.info
parameters.desc: Adds material names to extracted data.

parameters.name: region.info
parameters.desc: Adds region names to extracted data.

parameters.name: precision=
parameters.desc: Precision digits for floating values. Default: 6.

slice.example:
slice silicon y= 0.01
slice silicon value= 16.0

smooth.description: Smoothes a set of fields with specified smooth distances. Solves smoothing equations to reduce sharp gradients in fields such as implantation damage profiles.

parameters.name: smooth.field=
parameters.desc: List of fields to be smoothed.

parameters.name: smooth.distance=
parameters.desc: Smoothing distance for each field. Default: 2.0 nm.

parameters.name: init=
parameters.desc: First time step for solving smoothing equations. Default: 0.0001 s. Default unit: min.

solution.signature: solution [add] [damp] [DiffStep | GrowthStep | Heat | InitStep | smooth] [linear | logarithmic] [list] [material.list= <list>] [name=<c>] [needsolution] [negative] [nosolve | solve | ifpresent=<c>] [present] [reset] [store] [unit=<c>]
solution.description: Creates and modifies solution names for the Alagator diffusion equation system. Sets conditions for their inclusion in the matrix assembly, solver assignment, and extrapolation method. Solutions can be listed and checked for presence.

parameters.name: add
parameters.desc: Creates a new solution.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the solution variable.

parameters.name: damp
parameters.desc: Applies damping to the Newton iteration.

parameters.name: negative
parameters.desc: Allows the solution to have negative values.

parameters.name: solve
parameters.desc: Switches on solution status for the next command.

parameters.name: nosolve
parameters.desc: Prevents solving for this solution.

parameters.name: ifpresent=
parameters.desc: Solves only if all listed solutions are also present and being solved.

parameters.name: DiffStep
parameters.desc: Solved with the diffusion solver (dopants, defects).

parameters.name: GrowthStep
parameters.desc: Solved in the reaction step (oxidants).

parameters.name: Heat
parameters.desc: Solved during the laser annealing step.

parameters.name: InitStep
parameters.desc: Solved during the initialization step.

parameters.name: smooth
parameters.desc: Solved during smoothing steps.

parameters.name: linear
parameters.desc: Uses linear extrapolation for solution results.

parameters.name: logarithmic
parameters.desc: Uses logarithmic extrapolation for solution results.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Lists all currently defined solutions.

parameters.name: material.list=
parameters.desc: Materials where the solution variable will be solved.

parameters.name: unit=
parameters.desc: Unit of the solution variable (default: cm^-3).

parameters.name: present
parameters.desc: Returns true if the solution is defined and a data field matches.

parameters.name: store
parameters.desc: Stores the solution definition in a TDR file.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.desc: Resets reaction solution variables before diffusion (default: true).

solution.example:
solution name= Potential damp negative solve add
solution name= Vac !damp !negative solve add
solution name= I2 !damp !negative ifpresent= {Int Vac} add

sptopo.description: Alias for the topo command interface. Performs 2D or 3D etching and deposition using Sentaurus Topography 3D. Requires a Sentaurus Topography 3D license and compatible binary installation.

stdiff.description: Compares the current structure with one from a TDR file. Reads the external TDR file, interpolates data onto the current structure, and reports differences that exceed the relative error criteria.

parameters.name: <c>
parameters.desc: Full path or prefix of the TDR file to compare. The prefix is the filename without _fps.tdr.

strain_profile.description: Defines the strain introduced by an impurity as a piecewise linear function of the mole fraction in a given substrate. Used when materials such as germanium in silicon modify the lattice spacing.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Substrate material where the strains are defined.

parameters.name: species=
parameters.desc: Species in the substrate that cause the strain.

parameters.name: ratio=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of mole fraction values (0 to 1).

parameters.name: strain=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of strain values corresponding to the mole fractions.

strain_profile.example: strain_profile silicon species= Germanium ratio= {0 1} strain= {0 0.0425}

stress.description: Performs stress simulation and relaxation during process steps. Not documented as a standalone appendix command; stress calculations are typically invoked through the diffuse command with the stress.relax option or through the stressdata command for intrinsic stress definitions.

parameters.name: type=
parameters.desc: Type of stress simulation (e.g., viscous).

parameters.name: temperature=
parameters.desc: Temperature for stress calculation.

parameters.name: time=
parameters.desc: Duration for stress relaxation.

stressdata.signature: stressdata [?<material>? | region=<c> | region.list= {<c> ...}] [deposit.intrinsic] [sxxi=<n>] [syyi=<n>] [szzi=<n>] [sxyi=<n>] [syzi=<n>] [szxi=<n>] [bc.location= Left | Right | Front | Back | Bottom] [bc.value= {dx=<n> | dy=<n> | dz=<n> | pressure=<n> | rx=<n> | ry=<n> | rz=<n>}] [sxx | syy | szz | sxy | syz | szx | hs | pr | ps | vms] [number=<n>] [print.anisotropic.matrix] [apply.dislocation] [resolve.shear.stress] [optimize.dislocation] [saveTDR]
stressdata.description: Defines intrinsic stress of materials, sets boundary conditions for stress analysis, reports maximum stress values and locations, prints anisotropic material matrices, and defines edge dislocation settings. Zero is the default for all intrinsic stress parameters.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material for which stress parameters are set.

parameters.name: region=
parameters.desc: Region where stresses are applied.

parameters.name: region.list=
parameters.desc: List of regions for stress reporting.

parameters.name: deposit.intrinsic
parameters.desc: Defines intrinsic stress introduced during deposition.

parameters.name: sxxi=
parameters.desc: Intrinsic stress in xx-component (default unit: dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: syyi=
parameters.desc: Intrinsic stress in yy-component (default unit: dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: szzi=
parameters.desc: Intrinsic stress in zz-component (default unit: dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: sxyi=
parameters.desc: Intrinsic stress in xy-shear component (default unit: dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: syzi=
parameters.desc: Intrinsic stress in yz-shear component (default unit: dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: szxi=
parameters.desc: Intrinsic stress in zx-shear component (default unit: dyn/cm2).

parameters.name: bc.location=
parameters.desc: Boundary location: Left, Right, Front, Back, Bottom.

parameters.name: bc.value=
parameters.desc: Boundary condition: dx/dy/dz (displacement rate), pressure, rx/ry/rz (rotation).

parameters.name: sxx | syy | szz | sxy | syz | szx
parameters.desc: Reports maximum values for the specified stress component.

parameters.name: vms
