parameters.desc: Reports maximum von Mises stress (at nodes).

parameters.name: hs
parameters.desc: Reports maximum hydrostatic stress (at nodes).

parameters.name: pr
parameters.desc: Reports maximum pressure (at nodes).

parameters.name: ps
parameters.desc: Reports maximum principal stress (at element centroids).

parameters.name: number=
parameters.desc: Number of largest stress values to report.

parameters.name: print.anisotropic.matrix
parameters.desc: Prints the anisotropic material matrix.

parameters.name: apply.dislocation
parameters.desc: Applies edge dislocation settings.

parameters.name: saveTDR
parameters.desc: Saves results to a TDR file.

strip.description: Completely removes all exposed regions of the specified material from the top gas region. In 2D, the mesh is regenerated immediately. In 3D, only the boundary is modified until remeshing is needed.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: The material to completely remove from exposed surfaces.

parameters.name: remesh
parameters.desc: Controls whether to remesh after stripping in 2D. Default: true (remesh in 2D).

parameters.name: stress.relax
parameters.desc: Switches on stress relaxation after removal. Default: false.

struct.description: Writes the structure, simulation mesh, and field data to files in TDR format. Supports saving cross sections, boundary-only files, PDB parameters, and mesh command files for Sentaurus Mesh.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Controls whether adaptive meshing is used when saving 3D files. Default: pdbGet Grid Adaptive.

parameters.name: alt.maternames
parameters.desc: Writes alternative material names to the TDR file in addition to the Sentaurus Process material name.

parameters.name: compress.TDR
parameters.desc: Applies secondary compression to TDR files. Default: false.

parameters.name: contacts
parameters.desc: Writes contacts defined by the contact command into the boundary file. Default: true.

parameters.name: FullD
parameters.desc: Extrudes mesh to maximum simulation dimension before saving. Use !FullD to save in current dimension.

parameters.name: Gas
parameters.desc: Includes gas regions in TDR output. Use !Gas to exclude. Default: true.

parameters.name: interfaces
parameters.desc: Saves interface data in TDR files. Use !interfaces to prevent. Default: true.

parameters.name: mshcmd
parameters.desc: Writes a text _msh.cmd file with refinement info for Sentaurus Mesh alongside a TDR file.

parameters.name: pdb
parameters.desc: Saves PDB parameters along with geometry and data.

parameters.name: pdb.only
parameters.desc: Saves only PDB parameters without geometry and data.

parameters.name: sat
parameters.desc: Enforces or prevents saving of a Sentaurus Structure Editor restart file.

parameters.name: satfile=
parameters.desc: Defines the filename for the Sentaurus Structure Editor restart file. Default extension: .sat.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.desc: Specifies the coordinate scaling factor.

parameters.name: simplify=
parameters.desc: Maximum deviation of the simplified boundary from the extracted geometry for 2D/3D.

parameters.name: species=
parameters.desc: Names of data fields to write to TDR file. Empty list writes no data fields.

parameters.name: tdr=
parameters.desc: Saves a file in TDR format. Extension _fps.tdr is added automatically.

parameters.name: tdr.bnd=
parameters.desc: Writes a TDR file containing only the boundary representation.

parameters.name: tdr.spx=
parameters.desc: Writes a TDR file with GC representation for Sentaurus Process Explorer.

parameters.name: visual.1D
parameters.desc: Orders nodes for 1D visualization in Sentaurus Visual. Only nodal data stored.

parameters.name: x=
parameters.desc: X-coordinate for 2D cross section or 1D cutline. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: y=
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate for 2D cross section or 1D cutline. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Z-coordinate for 2D cross section or 1D cutline. Default unit: um.

struct.example:
struct tdr= n@node@_NMOS
struct tdr= output !Gas

substrate_profile.description: Defines the impurity profile of a species in a substrate in a piecewise linear manner. The piecewise linear function maps concentration values to corresponding x-coordinates.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Substrate material where the impurities are defined.

parameters.name: species=
parameters.desc: Name of the impurity species.

parameters.name: xcoord=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of x-coordinates where concentrations are defined (default unit: um).

parameters.name: concentration=
parameters.desc: Numeric list of impurity concentrations at the specified xcoord.

substrate_profile.example: substrate_profile Silicon species= Germanium xcoord= {0 0.01 0.011 0.5 0.7 10} concentration= {1e10 1e10 2e22 2e22 1e10 1e10}

tclsel.description: Selects the plot variable for postprocessing routines using Tcl expressions. A companion to the select command that accepts any general Tcl expression where existing data fields are available as Tcl variables. The expression is evaluated node-by-node. Useful for computing derived quantities such as logarithmic scales or differences from analytic profiles.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a list of currently defined data fields.

parameters.name: vec
parameters.desc: Lists vector data fields instead of real data fields.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material to which the command applies. Allows different expressions per material.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the new data field (default: Z_Plot_Var).

parameters.name: store
parameters.desc: Writes the data field to permanent storage in structure files (default: false).

parameters.name: z=
parameters.desc: Tcl expression for building the data field. Existing fields are Tcl variables; must be enclosed in braces.

temp_ramp.description: Defines a temperature profile for use with the diffuse command. Specifies multiple-step temperature ramps and holds, constructing complex temperature sequences. All gas_flow arguments are available. Referenced via the temp.ramp argument of the diffuse command.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the temperature ramp for reference in the diffuse command.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.desc: Clears the global list of temperature ramps.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Returns a list of defined temperature ramps.

parameters.name: temperature=
parameters.desc: Temperature value (default unit: degree Celsius).

parameters.name: time=
parameters.desc: Duration of this ramp segment (default unit: minute).

parameters.name: ramprate=
parameters.desc: Temperature change rate (default unit: K/s).

parameters.name: t.final=
parameters.desc: Final temperature for ramp (default unit: degree Celsius).

parameters.name: hold
parameters.desc: Holds the temperature at the specified value.

parameters.name: last
parameters.desc: Marks the last segment of a temperature ramp.

parameters.name: <ambient>
parameters.desc: Active ambient gas for oxidation during the ramp.

parameters.name: pressure=
parameters.desc: Total gas pressure (default: 1.0 atm).

parameters.name: epi.thickness=
parameters.desc: Epitaxial layer thickness to deposit during ramp.

parameters.name: delT=
parameters.desc: Maximum temperature step during ramp-up (default unit: degree Celsius).

parameters.name: delNT=
parameters.desc: Maximum temperature step during ramp-down (default unit: degree Celsius).

parameters.name: read.temp.file=
parameters.desc: Reads temperature profile from a file.

temp_ramp.example:
temp_ramp name= PWellrta time= 3.0<s> temperature= 600.0<C> ramprate= 150.0<K/s>
temp_ramp name= PWellrta time= 0.1<s> temperature= 1050.0<C>
temp_ramp name= PWellrta time= 6.0<s> temperature= 1050.0<C> ramprate=-75.0<K/s>

term.description: Creates, queries, and manages Alagator terms. A term is a named subexpression evaluated only once during assembly and cached for reuse across multiple equations. Terms provide computational benefit when an expression appears in several partial differential equations (e.g., a recombination term shared between vacancy and interstitial equations).

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the term.

parameters.name: add
parameters.desc: Add a term to the specified material.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Material name for the term (e.g., Silicon).

parameters.name: eqn=
parameters.desc: Expression defining the term. Braces delay variable expansion until diffuse execution.

parameters.name: print
parameters.desc: Print the equation for the term matching the specified name.

parameters.name: store
parameters.desc: Store the term in a TDR file.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: Return a list of all currently defined term names.

term.example: term name= MReact Silicon add eqn= "$k1 * ( M^2 - $k2 * M2)"

topo.description: Performs 2D or 3D etching and deposition using Sentaurus Topography 3D. All arguments are passed directly to the Sentaurus Topography 3D library, with automatic boundary exchange between Sentaurus Process and Topography 3D. Requires a Sentaurus Topography 3D license.

parameters.name: <Topo commands>
parameters.desc: Any Sentaurus Topography 3D commands (see Topography 3D User Guide).

topo.example:
topo define_deposit_machine model=simple material=Oxide rate=1 anisotropy=0.5
topo deposit spacing={0.01} time=0.1

transform.signature: transform (cut [location=<n>][<um>] (left | right | front | back | up | down) [max= {<n> <n> <n>}] [min= {<n> <n> <n>}] [mesh.align] [remesh] | flip [location=<n>] | reflect [keep.original] [merge.contacts] (left | right | front | back | up | down | ymin | ymax | zmin | zmax) | rotate [angle=<n>] [axis= "X" | "Y" | "Z"] | stretch (left | right | front | back | up | down) [length=<n>][<um>] [location=<n>][<um>] [remesh] | translate= {<n> <n> <n>}) [Adaptive] [masks]
transform.description: Cuts, flips, reflects, rotates, stretches, or translates a structure and currently defined refinement boxes. All transformations work in both 2D and 3D (except flip and stretch which are not available in KMC mode).

parameters.name: cut
parameters.desc: Crops the structure using max/min or location and a side.

parameters.name: flip
parameters.desc: Flips the structure from top to bottom at a specified x-location.

parameters.name: reflect
parameters.desc: Reflects the structure at the specified side.

parameters.name: rotate
parameters.desc: Rotates the structure by 90, 180, or 270 degrees around an axis.

parameters.name: stretch
parameters.desc: Stretches the structure at a given location along a side.

parameters.name: translate=
parameters.desc: Translation vector {dx dy dz}. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: location=
parameters.desc: Coordinate for cut/flip/stretch operations. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: length=
parameters.desc: Stretch length. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: angle=
parameters.desc: Rotation angle (90, 180, or 270 degrees).

parameters.name: axis=
parameters.desc: Rotation axis: X, Y, or Z.

parameters.name: keep.original
parameters.desc: Retains the original structure after reflection. Default: true.

parameters.name: merge.contacts
parameters.desc: Merges original and reflected contacts.

parameters.name: mesh.align
parameters.desc: Cuts at nearest mesh line. Set !mesh.align for precise cut with remesh. Default: true.

parameters.name: remesh
parameters.desc: Forces remesh after transformation (2D only).

parameters.name: masks
parameters.desc: Transforms all current masks together. Default: false.

parameters.name: Adaptive
parameters.desc: Switches on adaptive meshing if remesh is given.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: Maximum corner {maxx maxy maxz} for cut bounding box. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: Minimum corner {minx miny minz} for cut bounding box. Default unit: um.

transform.example:
transform reflect right
transform stretch location= 0.7 length= 0.02 right remesh
transform cut location= 0.7 left !remesh
transform translate= {-1 0 0}
transform rotate axis= "X" angle= 90

transform.mask.signature: transform.mask (cut | flip | reflect | rotate | stretch) [angle=<n>] [axis= "X" | "Y" | "Z"] [keep.original] [length=<n>][<um>] [location=<n>][<um>] (left | right | front | back | ymin | ymax | zmin | zmax) [max= {<n> <n> <n>}] [min= {<n> <n> <n>}] [name=<c>] [name.new=<c>] [translate= {<n> <n> <n>}]
transform.mask.description: Cuts, flips, reflects, rotates, stretches, or translates a given mask or all masks. A transformed mask replaces the old one by default. Use name.new to create a copy while retaining the original.

parameters.name: cut
parameters.desc: Crops the mask to a new bounding box or half.

parameters.name: flip
parameters.desc: Flips the mask (top to bottom).

parameters.name: reflect
parameters.desc: Reflects the mask at the specified side.

parameters.name: rotate
parameters.desc: Rotates the mask by a specified angle around an axis.

parameters.name: stretch
parameters.desc: Stretches the mask along a specified side.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the mask to transform.

parameters.name: name.new=
parameters.desc: Name of the transformed mask (creates a copy, keeps original).

parameters.name: keep.original
parameters.desc: Keeps original mask and combines with reflected mask. Default: true.

parameters.name: location=
parameters.desc: Coordinate for cut/flip/stretch operations. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: length=
parameters.desc: Stretch length. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: angle=
parameters.desc: Rotation angle.

parameters.name: axis=
parameters.desc: Rotation axis: X, Y, or Z.

parameters.name: translate=
parameters.desc: Translation vector {dx dy dz}. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: Maximum corner for cut bounding box. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: Minimum corner for cut bounding box. Default unit: um.

transform.mask.example:
transform.mask reflect right
transform.mask stretch location= 0.7 length= 0.02 right name= mask1 name.new= smask1
transform.mask translate= {-1 0 0} name= mask2 name.new= new2

transform.refinement.signature: transform.refinement (cut | flip | reflect | rotate | stretch) [angle=<n>] [axis= "X" | "Y" | "Z"] [keep.original] [length=<n>][<um>] [location=<n>][<um>] (left | right | front | back | up | down | ymin | ymax | zmin | zmax) [max= {<n> <n> <n>}] [min= {<n> <n> <n>}] [name=<c>] [name.new=<c>] [translate= {<n> <n> <n>}]
transform.refinement.description: Reflects, stretches, cuts, flips, rotates, or translates a given refinement box or all refinement boxes. Creates a new transformed refinement box by default while keeping the original. Override with !keep.original to transform in place.

parameters.name: cut
parameters.desc: Crops the refinement to a new bounding box or half.

parameters.name: flip
parameters.desc: Flips the refinement (top to bottom).

parameters.name: reflect
parameters.desc: Reflects the refinement at the specified side.

parameters.name: rotate
parameters.desc: Rotates the refinement by 90, 180, or 270 degrees.

parameters.name: stretch
parameters.desc: Stretches the refinement along a specified side.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement box to transform.

parameters.name: name.new=
parameters.desc: Name of the transformed refinement box.

parameters.name: keep.original
parameters.desc: Keeps the original refinement. Default: true.

parameters.name: location=
parameters.desc: Coordinate for cut/flip/stretch operations. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: length=
parameters.desc: Stretch length. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: angle=
parameters.desc: Rotation angle (90, 180, or 270 degrees).

parameters.name: axis=
parameters.desc: Rotation axis: X, Y, or Z.

parameters.name: translate=
parameters.desc: Translation vector {dx dy dz}. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: max=
parameters.desc: Maximum corner for cut bounding box. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: Minimum corner for cut bounding box. Default unit: um.

transform.refinement.example:
transform.refinement reflect right
transform.refinement stretch location= 0.7 length= 0.02 right name= box1 name.new= sbox1
transform.refinement cut location= 0.7 !keep.original

translate.description: Translates a named dataset with the specified offset vector. Spatially shifts a profile in one material or all materials. Points with undefined values after translation are filled with a minimum value.

parameters.name: <material>
parameters.desc: Limits translation to the specified material only. If omitted, translates in all materials.

parameters.name: name=
parameters.desc: Name of the dataset to translate. Default: Z_Plot_Var.

parameters.name: offset=
parameters.desc: Translation vector {dx dy dz}. Default unit: um.

parameters.name: min=
parameters.desc: Minimum value to fill undefined points. Default: 0.0.

update_principal_strain.description: Calculates the first, second, and third principal strains, and stores them as the fields PrincipalStrain1, PrincipalStrain2, and PrincipalStrain3, respectively. Not documented in the User Guide appendix; companion command to update_principal_stress.

update_principal_stress.description: Calculates the first, second, and third principal stresses, and stores them as the fields PrincipalStress1, PrincipalStress2, and PrincipalStress3, respectively.
